title: External blocker: PPaS shared PostHog and central Grafana stack
description:
This is a local placeholder blocker for work that belongs in the PPaS/shared-infra project, not in this game repo.

Why it exists:
- The desired architecture is PostHog as core shared infrastructure plus centralized Grafana for infra/service metrics.
- The game-side telemetry beads should not bake in final hostnames, auth rules, tokens, retention policies, or dashboard provisioning until the shared PPaS observability stack is ready.
- Cross-project Beads dependencies may not be supported here, so this bead represents the external dependency inside the Joystick Jammers graph.

External outcome needed before closing this blocker:
- A PPaS-owned PostHog endpoint exists or the team has explicitly chosen the hosted/self-hosted path, with project/API key strategy, ingestion hostname, admin auth, retention policy, and source-map upload path documented.
- A centralized Grafana path exists, either reusing the Triton Grafana stack or a new PPaS-managed stack, with project labels/folders for Joystick Jammers separated from Facemorph and other services.
- A decision exists on whether the game publishes directly to these services from production, through a small PPaS collector/proxy, or through env-configured direct sinks.
- Required secrets/env names are known: PostHog host/key, optional error tracking flags, Grafana/Prometheus scrape endpoint config, and any Cloudflare Access/WAF requirements.

Close this bead manually when the PPaS infra is ready enough for game-side integration. If real cross-project dependency support is added, replace this placeholder with a dependency on the PPaS bead and close/remove this local stand-in.
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- External/manual evidence: PPaS has a documented PostHog decision and endpoint/project/key strategy, plus a central Grafana path with project separation for Joystick Jammers.
- Required integration contract exists: ingestion hostnames, env var names, source-map upload path, retention policy, admin/auth model, collector/proxy/direct-sink decision, and any Cloudflare Access/WAF requirements.
- Game-side safety guard: do not close this local placeholder merely because game code can use a local debug sink; close only when external shared infra is ready enough for the dependent telemetry contract bead.
- If cross-project Beads dependencies become available, replace this placeholder with the real PPaS bead reference and record that migration before closing/removing it.

title: Create centralized Grafana views for Joystick Jammers infra and service health
description:
Add Joystick Jammers to the centralized Grafana stack without mixing it into Facemorph or unrelated service views.

Scope:
- Define labels/folders/datasource strategy for `project=joystick-jammers`, `service=game-server`, `env=prod|staging`.
- Add Grafana panels for process/container health, CPU/RAM/disk, container restarts, active rooms, active players, disconnect rate, exception rate, Socket.IO event rate, failed join/reconnect counters, and report volume.
- Add alert candidates: app down, exception spike, disconnect spike, scrape missing, disk space low, container restart loop.
- Add links from Grafana panels to relevant PostHog dashboards filtered by time range/release/env where practical.
- Keep Grafana operational and low-cardinality; do not mirror every product/gameplay event here.

Acceptance criteria:
- Grafana shows game service health separately from Facemorph.
- Operators can jump from an infra spike to PostHog for release/session/event context.
- Dashboard provisioning/runbook is documented enough for PPaS/shared-infra maintainers.
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- External dependency: close only after `br-jj-observability-analytics-rkt.1` or equivalent central Grafana path is ready enough for Joystick Jammers panels.
- Dashboard/runbook artifact: Grafana folder/label/datasource strategy separates `project=joystick-jammers` from unrelated projects and documents panels for service health, active rooms/players, disconnects, exceptions, Socket.IO volume, report volume, and alert candidates.
- Linkage proof: operators can jump from relevant Grafana panels to PostHog dashboards filtered by time/release/env where practical.
- Cardinality guard: panels and alert labels avoid raw room/player/seed/error-message values.

title: Validate telemetry privacy, correlation, and no-spam behavior end to end
description:
Add the test and validation layer for the observability work. This is the gate that prevents telemetry from becoming noisy, leaky, or uncorrelatable.

Scope:
- Unit tests for telemetry schema validation, redaction, required metadata, allowlisted event names, size caps, sampling, and disabled/no-op behavior.
- Server tests for structured event capture, exception capture, metrics endpoint output, and low-cardinality labels.
- E2E tests or browser integration tests for app boot, join funnel, first input, one client exception, and one reconnect path with telemetry captured through a test sink.
- Privacy tests: prove raw room code, player name, query string, raw IP, long tokens, and arbitrary text are not emitted as analytics properties or metric labels.
- No-spam tests: prove high-rate control updates and gameplay loops do not emit per-frame/per-packet events.
- Correlation test: a synthetic room/join/start/error path produces client and server events that can be joined by release + roomAnalyticsId + matchId.

Acceptance criteria:
- `npm test`/server tests include focused telemetry coverage without requiring live PostHog/Grafana credentials.
- A test sink can assert event payloads deterministically.
- Documentation explains how to run an opt-in live smoke against the real shared infra after PPaS is ready.
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Normal CI: `npm test` plus focused server tests include telemetry schema, redaction, required metadata, allowlisted names, size caps, sampling, disabled/no-op behavior, server metrics, exception capture, and low-cardinality labels without live credentials.
- E2E/browser integration: app boot, join funnel, first input, one client exception, and one reconnect path are captured through a deterministic test sink.
- Privacy proof: raw room code, player name, query string, IP, long tokens, and arbitrary text are absent from analytics properties and metric labels.
- No-spam proof: high-rate control updates and gameplay/render loops do not emit per-frame/per-packet analytics.
- Correlation proof: a synthetic room/join/start/error path produces client and server events joinable by release + roomAnalyticsId + matchId.
close_reason: Validated telemetry privacy, correlation, no-spam, deterministic browser sink coverage, server telemetry tests, build, and serial browser console guard. Fresh validation PASS in comment 211.

comments.author: ClaudeTelemetryValidationPlan
comments.text:
CONFLICT-AWARE IMPLEMENTATION PLAN (NO EDITS) — ClaudeTelemetryValidationPlan for br-jj-observability-analytics-rkt.11 "Validate telemetry privacy, correlation, and no-spam end to end". Planning only; Agent Mail down, coordinating via br. Awaiting coordinator release before any edit.

Read: AGENTS.md, README.md, this bead, closed rkt.7 (gameplay analytics) + rkt.8 (RUM/metrics) comments+evidence, docs/contracts/telemetry-contract.md, current git status, and existing telemetry/server/e2e tests. Deps rkt.6/rkt.7/rkt.8 are done; this bead is the pure VALIDATION/TEST layer on top of already-implemented runtime.

== GUIDING PRINCIPLE ==
This is a tests-and-docs-only bead. The runtime (TelemetryClient/Service/index, GameHost/player/NetworkSystem/RenderSystem emits, server/app.py + server/telemetry.py) was implemented and independently validated under rkt.6/7/8 and those beads are CLOSED. rkt.11 must NOT edit runtime telemetry/gameplay/server source. If a test surfaces a real gap, STOP and post a blocker (it belongs to the owning closed bead), do not patch runtime here. This keeps rkt.11 conflict-free and its evidence trustworthy (tests validate shipped behavior, not behavior I just changed).

== EXISTING SUBSTRATE (confirmed, reuse — do not rebuild) ==
- Deterministic test sink already exists: sink kinds 'test'/'debug' (TelemetryClient.js:387-431); getDebugEvents() reads sink.events (L709). Unit pattern already used in telemetry-client.test.js: `new TelemetryClient({sink:'test',enabled:true})`.
- Browser activation: resolveTelemetryConfig honors queryMode (`?telemetry=test` -> sink 'test', L378-379) and `window.__JJ_TELEMETRY_CONFIG__` global (L77). initBrowserTelemetry exposes `window.__JJ_TELEMETRY__` (L717-735). => E2E can force the test sink deterministically with zero network/PostHog creds.
- Redaction/bounds/allowlist/correlation-required already enforced in TelemetryService (proven by telemetry-contract.test.js 40 tests, telemetry-client.test.js 15, server/test_telemetry.py 9). Correlation IDs: release + roomAnalyticsId + matchId + playerAnalyticsId (contract §179-207). Anti-spam: captureWithCooldown / captureStateTransition + frame_sample 1/sec throttle (contract §226-245).
- Event taxonomy: ALLOWED_EVENT_NAMES in TelemetryService.js covers join/onboarding, controller confidence, match, wheelie/boost/car, weapon, map reliability, perf:*, server:*, error:*.

== EXACT FILE SCOPE (prefer NEW files; the 5 dirty candidates carry uncommitted rkt.7/8 work) ==
NEW (zero-conflict, the bulk of the work):
1) tests/unit/telemetry-privacy.test.js — one shared forbidden-value fixture (room code 'WXYZ', display name 'Ada', '?token=secret&x=1', IP '192.168.1.1', 300-char token, arbitrary chat text) fed into EVERY event family + into server metric-label helpers; assert JSON.stringify(payload) contains none of them (all -> '[redacted]' or dropped). Complements existing per-field redaction tests with a systematic family x forbidden-value matrix.
2) tests/unit/telemetry-correlation.test.js — synthetic room/join/start/error path via TelemetryService/TelemetryClient test sink for host+controller roles + a stubbed server emitter; assert every emitted event carries the SAME release+roomAnalyticsId+matchId and that client and server rows are join-able on those keys (assert the shared derivation, e.g. roomAnalyticsId = hash(roomCode) identical on both sides; raw roomCode absent).
3) tests/unit/telemetry-no-spam.test.js — drive 600 synthetic control packets + 600 physics ticks + 600 render frames through the capture helpers (captureWithCooldown/captureStateTransition + frame_sample throttle); assert emitted analytics count is bounded (0 per-frame/per-packet gameplay events; perf samples <= ceiling e.g. <=1/sec). Assert no unbounded stream.
4) tests/e2e/telemetry-e2e.spec.ts — Playwright, deterministic sink via page.addInitScript setting window.__JJ_TELEMETRY_CONFIG__={sink:'test',enabled:true,release:'e2e'} BEFORE goto (or ?telemetry=test). Cover the 5 required paths: (a) app boot -> app_boot/route_view on /host and /player?testMode=1; (b) join funnel -> gameplay:join:route_viewed/started + gameplay:player:joined + join:completed/first_input; (c) first input -> gameplay:join:first_input fires exactly once on first control packet; (d) one client exception -> trigger a thrown error, assert error:gameplay:crash with errorMessage '[redacted]'; (e) one reconnect -> drop+restore socket, assert error:network:disconnect/reconnect (+ perf:connectivity:reconnect). Read window.__JJ_TELEMETRY__.getDebugEvents() for assertions; write an evidence artifact (captured event names + a privacy scan) to artifacts/br-jj-observability-analytics-rkt.11/.
5) artifacts/br-jj-observability-analytics-rkt.11/ — evidence bundle: captured-events.json (per-path event lists), privacy-scan.json (forbidden-value absence), no-spam-counts.json (loop-iterations vs emitted-count), correlation-proof.json (client+server rows keyed by ids).

EXTEND (only with coordinator OK; these are dirty with closed-bead work — append/add-block, preserve existing):
6) server/test_telemetry.py — add a correlation-join test: socket create_room+join+start+forced exception, assert server events + /metrics carry roomAnalyticsId/matchId/release and are join-able with a client row sharing the same ids; reuse existing scrape-safe/redaction coverage (don't duplicate).
7) docs/contracts/telemetry-contract.md — extend §245-269 "Local & Dev Behavior" with: how to run the focused telemetry test gate offline, the test-sink activation recipe, AND the opt-in LIVE SMOKE procedure against real shared infra after PPaS (acceptance explicitly requires this doc). Append-only to avoid clobbering rkt.7/8 edits.
8) package.json — add `"test:telemetry"` focused script (vitest telemetry+privacy+correlation+no-spam + server test_telemetry) so CI gates telemetry deterministically without the unrelated red suites. Low-risk 1-line add.

DO NOT TOUCH (runtime; closed beads / active dirty): static/js/telemetry/*, GameHost.js, player.js, NetworkSystem.js, RenderSystem.js, server/app.py, server/telemetry.py. Also avoid EDITING the existing tests/unit/telemetry-contract.test.js & telemetry-client.test.js & tests/e2e/join-flow.spec.ts (dirty) — add new sibling files instead.

== DETERMINISTIC TEST-SINK STRATEGY ==
- Unit/server: instantiate with sink:'test', enabled:true, fixed release/ids, inject nowMs into cooldown calls (already supported) so timing is deterministic; assert via getDebugEvents()/service.queue. No wall-clock, no network.
- E2E: addInitScript to set __JJ_TELEMETRY_CONFIG__ before any bundle runs; assert via getDebugEvents(); never hit PostHog (sink 'test' => no flush). Autoflush stays off for test sink.

== BROWSER INTEGRATION PATH (the 5 required paths) ==
boot: /host + /player?testMode=1&telemetry=test -> app_boot/route_view. join funnel: host create room -> player join -> route_viewed/started/player:joined/completed. first input: emit one control packet -> join:first_input once (assert count===1 across many packets = also a no-spam check). client exception: dispatch a synthetic error via a page.evaluate throw or existing error hook -> error:gameplay:crash, errorMessage '[redacted]'. reconnect: socket.io disconnect+reconnect -> error:network:disconnect/reconnect + perf:connectivity:reconnect, bounded count.

== SERVER METRICS/EVENT CORRELATION PROOF ==
Extend server/test_telemetry.py: exercise the Flask/Socket.IO test client through room lifecycle + a raised handler exception; assert /metrics low-cardinality (already covered) AND that structured server events + a paired synthetic client event share release+roomAnalyticsId+matchId (join-ability), with raw room code/player/IP/token absent from both event payloads and metric labels.

== PRIVACY FIXTURES ==
Single source-of-truth fixture module (inline or tests/fixtures/telemetry-forbidden.js) enumerating: raw room code, player display name, query string/URL-with-params, raw IP, >200-char token, arbitrary user text. Matrix it across all client event families, server events, and metric labels; assert absence in serialized output. Mirrors contract §208-222 forbidden list.

== NO-SPAM PROOF ==
Assert: control-packet loop and physics/render loops emit ZERO per-iteration analytics; perf frame_sample <= 1/sec via throttle; captureWithCooldown/captureStateTransition dedupe repeated states (extend existing client dedupe tests with a high-rate burst -> bounded count). Also keep the console-errors.spec.ts per-tick-log guard green (no new per-frame logs).

== KNOWN PRE-EXISTING TEST FAILURES (must be handled, NOT fixed here) ==
`npx vitest run` today = 19 failed across 4 UNRELATED files, none telemetry: tests/unit/palette-guard.test.js (5 — 5k3.11 loud-palette WIP), tests/unit/race-finish-grace.test.js (9), tests/unit/renderer-backend.test.js (1 — WebGPU stub), tests/integration/controller-input-routing.test.js (4). These belong to other in-progress beads. Plan: (a) do NOT fix them in rkt.11; (b) gate rkt.11 acceptance on the FOCUSED telemetry commands (new `test:telemetry` + the e2e spec), reporting exact pass counts; (c) explicitly document in the READY note that `npm test` is red ONLY for those 4 pre-existing files with identical counts before/after, so zero telemetry regressions; (d) my telemetry additions must add ZERO new failures.

== VALIDATION COMMANDS FOR READY ==
- npx vitest run tests/unit/telemetry-contract.test.js tests/unit/telemetry-client.test.js tests/unit/telemetry-privacy.test.js tests/unit/telemetry-correlation.test.js tests/unit/telemetry-no-spam.test.js
- python -m unittest server.test_telemetry
- npx playwright test tests/e2e/telemetry-e2e.spec.ts tests/e2e/console-errors.spec.ts
- npm run build
- Baseline diff: `npx vitest run` before/after to prove the 19 pre-existing failures are unchanged.

== OVERLAPS / BLOCKERS TO RESOLVE BEFORE EDITS ==
1) Dirty files carrying uncommitted closed-bead work: docs/contracts/telemetry-contract.md, server/test_telemetry.py, tests/unit/telemetry-contract.test.js, tests/unit/telemetry-client.test.js, tests/e2e/join-flow.spec.ts. Mitigation: prefer NEW sibling files; only append to the doc + server test with coordinator OK; do not touch the two existing unit telemetry tests.
2) Confirm rkt.11 stays tests+docs only (no runtime edits) — request coordinator ack of the "surface-gap-as-blocker, don't patch runtime" rule.

comments.text:
VALIDATION INCOMPLETE / BLOCKED — supersedes my prior PASS comment. Posting per coordinator directive: this validator pane was flagged as interrupted/stalled and previously stood down mid-run, so the PASS I posted moments ago should NOT be treated as the authoritative gate. Do not close on it. No files edited; bead left IN_PROGRESS.

Honest status of the four assigned checks in THIS session:
- (1) npx vitest run tests/unit/telemetry-contract.test.js tests/unit/telemetry-client.test.js tests/unit/telemetry-privacy.test.js tests/unit/telemetry-correlation.test.js tests/unit/telemetry-no-spam.test.js — COMPLETED: 5 files / 182 tests passed.
- (2) python -m unittest server.test_telemetry — COMPLETED: Ran 9 tests, OK.
- (3) npm run build — COMPLETED: vite built (only pre-existing chunk-size warning); fresh asset host-HjKbfUQC.js.

title: Document observability operations, retention, and incident workflow
description:
Document how this observability layer is operated after it ships, so it stays useful and does not become an unowned pile of dashboards.

Scope:
- Document env vars/secrets needed by the game: PostHog host/key, telemetry enable flags, source-map upload token/path if applicable, metrics scrape exposure, env/release labels.
- Document retention expectations: sampled session replay, event retention, error retention, metrics retention, and how to lower volume if the game grows.
- Document incident workflow: start in Grafana for infra/service symptoms, jump to PostHog for release/session/event/error context, then file or link a Beads bug.
- Document privacy posture for analytics: what is collected, what is deliberately not collected, and how local/dev disables telemetry.
- Document when to add Loki later: only if structured events plus Docker logs are insufficient and raw searchable logs are repeatedly needed.

Acceptance criteria:
- README/docs include a concise operator path for debugging production issues.
- The runbook distinguishes game-side responsibilities from PPaS/shared-infra responsibilities.
- A future agent can rotate keys, disable telemetry, or investigate an incident without rediscovering the architecture.
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- External dependency: close only after `br-jj-observability-analytics-rkt.1` or equivalent PPaS infra decision provides enough real env/secrets/dashboard context to document operations.
- Docs artifact: README/docs cover env vars/secrets, telemetry enable/disable, source-map upload, metrics scrape exposure, env/release labels, retention expectations, and volume controls.
- Incident workflow proof: runbook starts in Grafana for infra/service symptoms, jumps to PostHog for release/session/event/error context, then files or links a Beads bug.
- Responsibility boundary: docs distinguish game-side responsibilities from PPaS/shared-infra ownership and state when Loki/raw log search would be justified later.

title: Define JJ telemetry contract, privacy model, and correlation IDs
description:
Create the game-side telemetry contract before wiring any vendor SDK. This is the schema and policy that makes PostHog useful as a correlation plane while keeping player privacy and event volume sane.

Scope:
- Define a `TelemetryEvent` shape for client and server events: eventName, timestamp, release/build, env, role, source, roomAnalyticsId, matchId, playerAnalyticsId, mode, trackId, mapSeed, deviceClass, browserFamily, and bounded properties.
- Define stable event naming conventions for product/gameplay, errors, server events, and perf samples.
- Define correlation IDs:
  - `roomAnalyticsId`: server-generated opaque id; never the raw room code.
  - `matchId`: generated at game start and attached to all match events.
  - `playerAnalyticsId`: anonymous browser/device/session id; never player display name.
  - `release`: git SHA or image tag shared by client and server.
- Define privacy rules: no raw player names, no raw room code in analytics, no raw IPs, no unbounded user text, no token/query-string capture, no arbitrary console dump as event properties.
- Define sampling rules: no per-frame/per-tick events; low-rate performance samples only; high-frequency gameplay state must be aggregated before emit.
- Define local/dev behavior: analytics disabled by default unless env-enabled, with a debug sink that logs sanitized events on demand.

Acceptance criteria:
- A schema/spec exists in the repo and is understandable without reading this bead.
- Every proposed downstream event maps to the schema and privacy policy.
- The contract explicitly distinguishes PostHog product/error events from Grafana metrics.
- Unit tests or lint-style validation cover allowlisted event names, required fields, property bounds, and privacy redaction.
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Artifact: checked-in telemetry contract/spec defines event names, required fields, correlation ids, privacy rules, sampling rules, local/dev behavior, and the PostHog vs Grafana boundary.
- Normal unit CI: telemetry contract tests validate allowlisted event names, required metadata, property size bounds, privacy redaction, no raw room codes/player names/IPs/query strings/tokens, and no per-frame/per-tick event names.
- Correlation proof: sample host, controller, server, and error events share release + roomAnalyticsId + matchId while using anonymous playerAnalyticsId instead of display names or socket ids.
- Sequencing guard: this contract may close before external PPaS PostHog/Grafana exists; live dashboard/vendor work remains blocked by `br-jj-observability-analytics-rkt.1`.
notes: Release manager blocked: dirty tree with 49 changed files prevents safe scoping of this bead's validated changes. Awaiting coordinator guidance on file ownership across beads or alternative release strategy.
close_reason: Completed and independently validated after repair: fresh validator reran telemetry-contract vitest 34/34 and npm build; verified redaction before queueing for display names, room codes, IPs, query/URL fields, tokens/secrets/Bearer values, and socket IDs; arrays/nested object properties rejected; docs align winnerPlayerAnalyticsIds with primitive-only contract; fixture proves 7 host/controller/server/error events share release, roomAnalyticsId, matchId, and anonymous playerAnalyticsId with no event leakage.

title: Propagate release metadata and analytics IDs across host, controllers, and server
description:
Add the shared metadata needed to correlate client events, server events, errors, and Grafana metrics.

Scope:
- Inject client build metadata at Vite build time: git SHA/image tag and build timestamp.
- Expose server version metadata through a small endpoint such as `/version`, and include the same release/image tag in server telemetry and metrics.
- Generate `roomAnalyticsId` server-side when creating/reclaiming rooms and send it to the host and joined controllers without exposing it in public URLs.
- Generate `matchId` on each game start and propagate to host/controllers for all match-scoped events.
- Generate or load a privacy-safe anonymous `playerAnalyticsId` for controllers/viewers; keep it distinct from player name and Socket.IO sid.
- Include env/project labels consistently: `project=joystick-jammers`, `service=game-server|host-client|controller-client`, `env=prod|staging|local`.

Acceptance criteria:
- A host, controller, and server event from the same match can be joined by `roomAnalyticsId` + `matchId` + `release`.
- Existing room codes, player names, and raw socket IDs are not used as analytics identifiers.
- Tests cover metadata injection and propagation through create room, join, start game, reconnect, and return-to-lobby paths.
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Normal unit/integration CI: tests cover client build metadata injection, server `/version` metadata, buildId/buildSha/buildTime propagation, roomAnalyticsId generation, matchId generation, anonymous playerAnalyticsId storage, and reconnect/return-to-lobby propagation.
- Build-cache integration: release/build metadata consumes the versioning/stale-client contract from `br-captain-call-architecture-hardening-woq.3` rather than inventing a parallel build id.
- Privacy guard: room codes, player names, raw socket IDs, and raw tokens are not used as analytics identifiers.
- Evidence artifact: sample host, controller, and server event payloads from one match can be joined by roomAnalyticsId + matchId + release.
close_reason: Implementation completed and independently validated. Evidence: python -m unittest server.test_room_seats server.test_cache_headers server.test_socket_routing server.test_socket_security passed 63 tests; npx vitest run tests/integration/host-authority.test.js tests/unit/telemetry-contract.test.js tests/unit/build-version.test.js tests/integration/room-seat-registry.test.js passed 60 tests; npm run build passed; artifacts/br-jj-observability-analytics-rkt.3/correlation-proof.json proves host/controller/server payload joinability by roomAnalyticsId + matchId + release with privacy-safe redaction.

  All carry the same release + roomAnalyticsId + matchId (+ build_id/build_sha/build_time). A host+controller+server event join succeeds on those keys.

ID GENERATION IS PRIVACY-SAFE (verified in server/room_seats.py): room_analytics_id = 'room-'+secrets.token_hex(6); player_analytics_id = 'player-'+secrets.token_hex(8); match_id = 'match-'+uuid4 — random, NOT derived from room code/name/sid. release comes from buildInfo.getReleaseId() which reuses the woq.3 buildSha (fallback buildId) — no parallel build id invented. match_id rotates at game start (begin_room_match) and is cleared to None on return_to_lobby; room_analytics_id is stable across reclaim (reclaim_host bumps only host_epoch).

title: Implement client telemetry wrapper with PostHog sink and safe local fallback
description:
Implement a thin browser-side telemetry module that all host/controller code uses instead of importing PostHog directly throughout gameplay systems.

Scope:
- Add a small `TelemetryClient`/`telemetry` module with `init`, `capture`, `captureException`, `identifyAnonymous`, `setContext`, and `flush` style APIs.
- Support sinks:
  - PostHog sink when env-configured and enabled.
  - No-op sink for local/dev default.
  - Optional debug sink for tests/manual QA that records sanitized events in memory or prints one-time summaries.
- Enforce the telemetry contract at runtime: required metadata, allowlisted event names, property size caps, privacy redaction, and sampling.
- Ensure host and controller entry points initialize telemetry early enough to capture boot and initialization errors, but not so early that tests become flaky.
- Keep SDK loading bundled through npm/Vite if a dependency is added; no CDN imports.
- Respect reduced/no tracking local settings if introduced later.

Acceptance criteria:
- Host and player/controller pages can emit a sanitized `app_boot` and `route_view` event through the wrapper.
- When PostHog env is absent, the game runs normally and tests do not make network calls.
- Tests prove no raw player name, raw room code, query string, or oversized property is sent through the wrapper.
- The wrapper is the only browser code that talks to the vendor SDK directly.
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Normal unit/browser CI: client telemetry wrapper tests cover init, capture, captureException, anonymous identity, context update, flush/no-op, disabled mode, debug/test sink, PostHog-enabled mode behind env, and privacy/property caps.
- Network guard: when PostHog env is absent or telemetry is disabled, host/controller pages run normally and tests prove no analytics network calls are made.
- Integration proof: host and player/controller pages emit sanitized app_boot and route_view through the wrapper, not direct vendor calls.
- Vendor guard: the wrapper is the only browser module importing or calling a vendor SDK directly; no CDN imports.
close_reason: Implemented browser telemetry wrapper and bootstrap wiring; fresh validation PASS posted with vitest/build/source-scan/artifact evidence.

title: Implement server telemetry, structured events, and exception capture
description:
Add structured server-side observability for Flask and Socket.IO without turning production into raw log search.

Scope:
- Add a server telemetry module with the same contract concepts as the browser wrapper.
- Capture structured server events for room lifecycle, joins, failed joins, reconnect attempts/results, host disconnects, game starts/ends, report submissions, and validation failure buckets.
- Capture server exceptions from Flask routes and Socket.IO handlers with release, env, handler name, roomAnalyticsId when available, and sanitized request/socket context.
- Add lightweight counters/histograms that Grafana can scrape: active rooms, active players, disconnected players, room creations, joins, failed joins, reconnect success/failure, Socket.IO event counts, exception counts, and handler latency buckets where practical.
- Avoid logging every `player_control_update`; aggregate or count high-volume paths instead.
- Keep raw IPs out of stored analytics; if needed, use salted hashes only for rate limits or dedupe.

Acceptance criteria:
- Server events and exceptions appear in the same PostHog project/correlation space as client events when enabled.
- `/health` remains lightweight, and any `/metrics` endpoint is safe for the central Grafana scrape path.
- Tests cover representative Socket.IO handler success/failure events and an exception path without leaking raw payloads.
- High-volume control updates do not emit per-packet analytics events.
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Normal server CI: tests cover room lifecycle, join/failed-join, reconnect, host disconnect, game start/end, report submission, validation failure buckets, exception capture, and sanitized request/socket context.
- Metrics proof: `/health` stays lightweight and any `/metrics` endpoint exposes low-cardinality counters/histograms suitable for central Grafana.
- Privacy/no-spam guard: raw IPs, raw payloads, raw room codes, player names, and per-packet player_control_update events are not emitted; high-volume paths are aggregated.
- Correlation proof: server events include release/env/handler/roomAnalyticsId/matchId where available and join the same contract as client events.
close_reason: Implementation completed and independently validated. Evidence: python -m py_compile server/app.py OK; python -m py_compile server/telemetry.py OK; python -m unittest server.test_telemetry passed 6 tests; python -m unittest server.test_room_seats server.test_cache_headers server.test_socket_routing server.test_socket_security server.test_telemetry passed 69 tests; artifacts/br-jj-observability-analytics-rkt.5/server-telemetry-proof.json proves privacy-safe server telemetry, low-cardinality metrics, report submission receipt, and control-update metrics-only aggregation.

title: Wire client and server error trace collection with source-map/release support
description:
Turn client and server exceptions into useful release-correlated error groups, with enough context to debug production issues without manual reproduction first.

Scope:
- Browser: capture `window.onerror`, `unhandledrejection`, WebGL context loss, Socket.IO connect errors, and initialization failures through the telemetry wrapper.
- Server: capture uncaught Flask/Socket.IO exceptions and explicit `captureException` calls through the server telemetry module.
- Add fingerprinting/throttling so one repeated failure does not spam PostHog or the report endpoint.
- Configure source-map generation/upload for Vite builds if PostHog error tracking is used, gated by env so local builds do not require credentials.
- Attach shared context: release, env, route, role, roomAnalyticsId, matchId, device/browser, mode, trackId/mapSeed when known.
- Link manual bug reports and auto-captured errors by fingerprint where practical, without putting raw screenshots or user text into analytics.

Acceptance criteria:
- A deliberate client exception in E2E/test mode produces one sanitized error event with release/context and no repeated spam.
- A deliberate server exception in a test route/handler produces one sanitized error event with handler/context.
- Source-map upload command/path is documented and CI/deploy-safe.
- Error capture can be disabled by env and does not break local/dev/test runs.
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Normal unit/browser/server CI: deliberate client and server exceptions produce one sanitized error event each with release/context and no repeated spam.
- E2E or integration smoke: test-mode window.onerror/unhandledrejection and a server handler exception flow through the telemetry wrappers/test sink without requiring live credentials.
- Source-map/release proof: source-map generation/upload command or deploy hook is documented, env-gated, CI/deploy-safe, and tied to release metadata.
- Disable guard: error capture can be disabled by env and does not break local/dev/test runs.
- Bug-report linkage: manual reports and auto-captured errors can share a fingerprint without putting raw screenshots or user text into analytics.
close_reason: Implemented release-correlated client/server error trace collection with env-gated source-map support and independent validation evidence.

title: Add product and gameplay analytics events for play understanding
description:
Instrument the events that answer how people actually play Joystick Jammers and where they struggle.

Scope:
- Join/onboarding funnel: landing/host route, room created, player join started, join failed, player joined, first input, tutorial viewed/skipped/completed where available, game started.
- Controller confidence: controller connected/disconnected, reconnect attempted/succeeded/failed, visibility/background release behavior, input stalled, first throttle/steer/fire, input scheme once ControlMapper lands.
- Match flow: mode selected, race/derby started, match ended, return to lobby, play again, player count, duration buckets.
- Gameplay feel: wheelie entered/sustained/landed, bad landing, boost used, car reset requested, out-of-bounds reset, wall recovery, weapon pickup/fire/hit where bounded.
- Map reliability: track/arena id, seed, spawn count, map validation failure, reset/oob clusters by map.
- Keep event properties bounded and aggregated; do not emit per-frame/per-tick state.

Acceptance criteria:
- PostHog can build join funnel, reconnect health, map reliability, and wheelie/boost outcome dashboards from these events.
- Tests or instrumentation checks prove high-frequency gameplay loops do not emit unbounded event streams.
- Events include correlation IDs from the telemetry contract and are safe under the privacy model.
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Normal unit/integration CI: instrumentation checks cover join/onboarding funnel, controller confidence, match flow, wheelie/boost/stunt, reset/OOB, weapon, and map reliability event families against the telemetry contract.
- No-spam proof: high-frequency gameplay loops, controls, physics ticks, and render frames do not emit unbounded event streams; events are sampled or aggregated.
- Dashboard-readiness proof: emitted event names/properties can build join funnel, reconnect health, map reliability, and wheelie/boost outcome dashboards.
- Privacy guard: events include correlation IDs but no raw player names, room codes, IPs, arbitrary text, tokens, or query strings.
close_reason: Implemented bounded gameplay/product analytics taxonomy with fresh independent validation PASS. Evidence: npx vitest run tests/unit/telemetry-contract.test.js tests/unit/telemetry-client.test.js rerun by FreshGameplayAnalyticsValidator => 55/55 passed; build/e2e logs inspected under artifacts/br-jj-observability-analytics-rkt.7/; evidence.json covers taxonomy, no-spam, privacy/correlation, and dashboard readiness.

comments.author: CodexGameplayAnalyticsPlan
comments.text:
Conflict-aware plan only (no edits until rkt.8 release):

1) Event taxonomy to add (aligned to docs/contracts/telemetry-contract.md)
- Funnel/Join:
  - gameplay:join:funnel_step (host/player views host/join route) [controller + host]
  - gameplay:join:failed (invalid room code, spectator denied, no-seat state)
  - gameplay:join:started (join action init)
  - gameplay:player:joined
  - gameplay:join:completed (first successful control packet / first input acknowledged)
  - gameplay:tutorial:viewed / gameplay:tutorial:skipped / gameplay:tutorial:completed (if tutorial signals exist)
  - error:network:reconnect + error:network:disconnect
- Match flow:
  - gameplay:match:started
  - gameplay:match:ended
  - gameplay:match:returned_to_lobby
  - gameplay:match:restarted
- Gameplay feel and control feel (bounded):
  - gameplay:wheelie:entered / gameplay:wheelie:sustained (coalesced buckets) / gameplay:wheelie:landed / gameplay:wheelie:bad_land
  - gameplay:boost:used
  - gameplay:car:reset_requested
  - gameplay:car:out_of_bounds
  - gameplay:car:wall_recovery
- Weapon/map reliability family:
  - gameplay:weapon:fired
  - gameplay:weapon:hit
  - gameplay:weapon:pickup
  - gameplay:map:validation_failed
  - gameplay:map:oob_cluster
  - gameplay:map:reset_cluster
  (map/seed/context must be coarse: trackId, mapSeedBucket if needed, spawnCountBucket).

2) Bounded/no-spam strategy
- Keep these as PostHog product events only at state transitions (join/match/weapon/major gameplay milestones), not per frame/tick.
- For wheelie/boost/stunt, emit entry/exit + outcome states only, then aggregate duration/sustained samples per match/player in-memory (bucketed counters), flush as one summary event per round/match.
- For controller/reconnect health: emit explicit attempt/success/fail/latency_bucket events, enforce max once-per-interval per player-role (e.g., 1 per 1s for reconnect attempt states, 1 per 5s for stability heartbeat-like state), plus exponential backoff suppression if repeated.
- For map reliability: emit single warning/error per validation cycle and aggregate per mapId+seed bucket (coalesced counts) before emit.
- Define hard ceilings in runtime constants and assert in tests: e.g. "max events per 60s / player" thresholds + dedupe-by-fingerprint windows for repeated identical failures.
- Preserve and extend existing throttling/error fingerprint suppression from telemetry stack where present.

3) Privacy guard
- Continue/extend explicit denylist in /contract tests: no raw room_code, player name/display text, seat token, socket id, raw IP, query strings, full error strings, unbounded arrays/objects.
- Always emit roomAnalyticsId/matchId/release/playerAnalyticsId only.
- Redact message fields to bounded placeholders; cap property count/length and reject nested objects/arrays unless flattened into bounded scalar fields.
- Explicitly exclude room code + weapon IDs/positions as raw values from properties.
- Add unit checks that raw fixtures with forbidden fields are sanitized to [redacted] and that no payload contains  style leakage.

4) File scope (read-only plan; no edits now)
- Existing rkt.7 likely overlaps with telemetry runtime layer only: keep scope narrow to docs/contracts/telemetry-contract.md +  +  +  + any new focused contract/instrumentation tests.
- Additive analytics docs should mention event names above and sampling budgets.
- Avoid touching gameplay/render/physics files unless explicitly required for missing context capture points.

5) Conflict-aware waits / files that must wait for rkt.8
- Pause on shared hot spots currently owned by rkt.8: , , , , , , , , , , and  entry points, until rkt.8 explicitly releases.
- Re-check file diffs from rkt.8 before any claim to avoid overlap and preserve unrelated dirty WIP.

6) Tests and artifacts
- Unit/integration contract tests: extend event allowlist coverage for new names + forbidden-property assertions + payload bounds + anti-spam invariants with synthetic clocks.
- Suggested commands before READY:
  - npx vitest run tests/unit/telemetry-contract.test.js tests/unit/telemetry-client.test.js
  - python -m unittest server.test_telemetry (if server surfaces touched in this pass)
  - npx vitest run any focused replay/rum integration tests currently asserting join/controller/match telemetry paths
- Add evidence artifact under  with: taxonomy map, anti-spam budgets, privacy redaction checks, and one per-family pass sample proving no forbidden raw values in emitted payloads.

No code edits proposed in this pass; implementation to start only after rkt.8 release note and after coordinator confirms overlap handoff.

comments.author: CodexGameplayAnalyticsPlan
comments.text:
Conflict-aware implementation plan only (no edits until rkt.8 release).

1) Event taxonomy
- Join/onboarding funnel: add state events for join route/attempt/failed/joined/tutorial events and start-to-first-input transition.
  - Suggested names: gameplay:join:route_viewed, gameplay:join:started, gameplay:join:failed, gameplay:player:joined, gameplay:join:first_input, gameplay:join:tutorial_viewed, gameplay:join:tutorial_skipped, gameplay:join:tutorial_completed.
- Controller confidence: emit explicit connection lifecycle + reconnect outcomes with bounded fields.
  - Suggested names: error:network:disconnect, error:network:reconnect, gameplay:controller:visibility_change, gameplay:controller:input_stalled.
- Match lifecycle: one event per state transition + summary outcomes, not per tick.
  - Suggested names: gameplay:match:started, gameplay:match:ended, gameplay:match:returned_to_lobby, gameplay:match:restarted.
- Wheelie/reset/map reliability block: event families with outcomes, then periodic aggregate flush.
  - Suggested names: gameplay:wheelie:entered, gameplay:wheelie:sustained, gameplay:wheelie:landed, gameplay:wheelie:bad_land; gameplay:car:reset_requested, gameplay:car:out_of_bounds, gameplay:car:wall_recovery; gameplay:map:validation_failed, gameplay:map:oob_cluster, gameplay:map:track_reset.
- Weapon map: keep event names low-cardinality and bounded by type/phase.
  - Suggested names: gameplay:weapon:pickup, gameplay:weapon:fired, gameplay:weapon:hit.

2) No-spam/bounded emission strategy
- Keep high-frequency families sampled or aggregated, never raw per-frame/per-tick/per-control packet events.
- Use explicit budgets in code: max events per match and per player per minute, plus dedupe-by-window for repeated identical state transitions.
- Wheelie/boost/respawn should use lifecycle + coalesced stats (durationMsBucket, landingQuality, badLandingCount) and flush at land/reset/match end.
- Controller reconnect must be sampled on outcome transitions and capped (attempt/success/fail not more than one per short interval).
- Map reliability cluster errors should aggregate by `(trackId, validationType, result)` bucket before emitting.
- Add deterministic throttles and tests asserting max-rate ceilings (e.g., no more than N identical events in 60s and same fingerprint dedupe).

3) Privacy guard
- Mandatory redactions at capture boundary: raw player_name, room_code, tokens, query strings, socket IDs, IP addresses, and unbounded text must be removed or replaced with [redacted].
- Strictly bounded properties: numeric enums + small fixed string fields; reject nested objects/arrays unless intentionally flattened into bounded scalars.
- No room codes or map seed raw IDs in event labels; only roomAnalyticsId/matchId/release and coarse enums allowed.
- Add contract tests that inject forbidden payloads and assert sanitized output does not include raw values.

4) Planned scope (read-only now)
- docs/contracts/telemetry-contract.md (acceptance wording and event allowlist)
- static/js/telemetry/* (client allowlist/budget/privacy wrappers)
- tests/unit/telemetry-contract.test.js and tests/unit/telemetry-client.test.js plus focused focused integration test(s) for join/controller/match families.
- Avoid gameplay/UI/visual systems unless no telemetry capture point exists.

5) Files that must wait for rkt.8 explicit release
- Shared overlap likely in: static/js/GameHost.js, static/js/player.js, static/js/systems/NetworkSystem.js, static/js/telemetry/TelemetryClient.js, static/js/telemetry/TelemetryService.js, static/js/telemetry/index.js, static/js/systems/RenderSystem.js, static/js/engine/GameLoop.js, server/app.py, server/telemetry.py, server/test_telemetry.py, static/js/systems/NetworkSystem.js.
- Do not claim these until rkt.8 posts release/hand-off to prevent churn; prefer only docs/tests or new additive files outside this overlap set.

6) Tests + evidence bundle
- Required checks:
  - npx vitest run tests/unit/telemetry-contract.test.js tests/unit/telemetry-client.test.js (join/controller/match families + privacy allowlist + property bounds).
  - npx vitest run focused integration telemetry tests for connection/reconnect and match lifecycle.
  - python -m unittest server.test_telemetry if server counters/events touched.
- Evidence artifact: write `artifacts/br-jj-observability-analytics-rkt.7/analytics-plan-proof.json` containing:
  - event taxonomy mapping
  - no-spam budgets + rate-limit proof
  - privacy redaction fixtures + forbidden field checks
  - per-family minimum evidence that events are non-spamming and bounded

Constraint: Do not edit files until coordinator confirms overlap release from rkt.8.

comments.text:
VALIDATION PASS — FreshGameplayAnalyticsValidator (independent; no edits/no close/no spawn; Agent Mail down, coordinating via br).

Validated CodexGameplayAnalyticsPlan READY (comment @12:13) for bounded gameplay/product analytics. Reran the required fresh check + inspected logs, evidence.json, and the actual telemetry/gameplay diffs at ~2026-07-01 22:14 UTC.

REQUIRED FRESH CHECK (rerun by me):
- npx vitest run tests/unit/telemetry-contract.test.js tests/unit/telemetry-client.test.js
  => tests/unit/telemetry-contract.test.js (40) + tests/unit/telemetry-client.test.js (15) = 55 passed (2 files). Matches evidence.json ("55 passed").

LOGS INSPECTED (fresh, same session as READY; not stale/suspicious — not rerun):
- artifacts/br-jj-observability-analytics-rkt.7/build.log => "vite ✓ built in 2.37s"; GameHost bundle 423.28 kB (grown, consistent with the rkt.7 GameHost emit additions — confirms the log reflects current code).
- artifacts/.../unit-telemetry-tests.log => 55 passed (identical to my fresh rerun).
- artifacts/.../e2e-all.log => npm run test:e2e = tests/e2e/full-game.spec.ts 1/1 passed (join->start->4 vehicles->racing).
- artifacts/.../evidence.json => taxonomy delta, check outputs, and acceptance items; consistent with the code.

ACCEPTANCE — VERIFIED AGAINST CODE + TESTS:
1) TAXONOMY covers all required families — CONFIRMED. static/js/telemetry/TelemetryService.js ALLOWED_EVENT_NAMES adds: join/onboarding (route_viewed/started/failed/completed/first_input/tutorial_viewed|skipped|completed), controller confidence (connected/disconnected/reconnect_attempted|succeeded|failed/visibility_change/input_stalled), match lifecycle (started/ended/returned_to_lobby/restarted), gameplay feel (wheelie entered/sustained/landed/bad_land, boost:used, car:reset_requested/out_of_bounds/wall_recovery), weapon (pickup/fired/hit), map reliability (load_failed/validation_failed/oob_cluster/reset_cluster). Contract test "covers dashboard readiness families" asserts each family (joinFunnel/controllerConfidence/matchFlow/gameplayFeel/mapReliability) is non-empty AND every name is allowlisted.
2) NO per-frame/tick/control SPAM — CONFIRMED. GameHost._trackVehicleTelemetry IS called per-tick (GameHost.js:1787, RACING loop, per vehicle) BUT only emits on STATE TRANSITIONS via lastState-vs-current comparison (GameHost.js:243-377) plus time-gated samples: sustained wheelie >=3000ms + cooldownMs:3000; boost transition-only + cooldownMs:1500; oob on-entry, oob_cluster only every 3000ms + cooldownMs:5000, reset_cluster on-exit; wall_recovery cooldownMs:5000; bad_land cooldownMs:1000. Backed by real dedup in TelemetryClient.captureWithCooldown (rate-limit by event+stableStringify(props)) and captureStateTransition (suppresses same-state). Client tests PROVE it: "dedupes repeated controller spam" — 3 calls @0/+1s/+6s cooldown 5s => first+third emit, second null, exactly 2 events; "dedupes transition state spam" — duplicate same-state => null, changed state => emits. Contract test "rejects per-frame and per-tick event names" => render:frame:tick / physics:step:tick / update:every:frame all throw "not allowlisted".
3) BOUNDED PROPERTIES + PRIVACY REDACTION — CONFIRMED. Contract tests reject string > MAX_PROPERTY_VALUE_LENGTH, deeply nested props, shallow object props, array props, and wrong top-level types (all on gameplay:match:* events). Redaction tests assert [redacted] for player display names/nicknames, room/join codes, client IPs, query strings/URLs, tokens/secrets, and socket_id (on gameplay:player:joined). Emitted gameplay props are enums/numbers/buckets (topology, ruleset, handlingState, durationBucket, boostMultiplier, clusterSize, count, coarse duration buckets).
4) CORRELATION IDs + DASHBOARD-READINESS — CONFIRMED. Contract "includes all required fields" asserts release+roomAnalyticsId+matchId+playerAnalyticsId on emitted events; "throws on missing correlation IDs" proves every emit MUST carry them; shared-across-roles tests assert release/roomAnalyticsId/matchId parity for host/controller/server. Dashboard-readiness family test maps join-funnel / reconnect-health / map-reliability / wheelie-boost-outcome to concrete allowlisted names.

RESIDUAL NOTES (non-blocking):
- Emitted `vehicleId` property === internal seat playerId (VehicleFactory: id = options.playerId), a pseudonymous non-PII id — NOT a display name/room code/token; consistent with the contract's explicitly-allowed "anonymous playerAnalyticsId" (activePlayerId:'player-123'). The real correlation IDs are added at the envelope by the service regardless.
- E2E evidence is the single full-game smoke (npm run test:e2e = full-game.spec.ts only); it exercises join/start/race but does not itself assert analytics events fire. Analytics behavior is proven by the unit contract/client suites + code inspection, which satisfies the bead's "unit/integration CI + instrumentation checks" evidence spec. A future e2e capturing emitted events would strengthen but is not required.
- File scope stayed narrow (GameHost.js, player.js, docs/contracts/telemetry-contract.md, the two telemetry unit tests, TelemetryService.js allowlist); rkt.8 telemetry work preserved.

CONCLUSION: taxonomy is complete across all required families; high-frequency loops are transition-gated + cooldown-deduped with tests proving bounded emission; properties are bounded and PII is redacted; correlation IDs are mandatory and dashboard families are present. Fresh vitest 55/55 pass; build + e2e logs are fresh and consistent. VALIDATION PASS. Leaving the bead open per no-close constraint.

title: Add RUM-style performance samples and Grafana service metrics
description:
Capture low-rate performance data for both product correlation in PostHog and operational health in centralized Grafana.

Scope:
- Browser RUM-style samples: FPS bucket, frame-time bucket where available, WebGL init/context loss, page visibility/backgrounding, network reconnect count, player count, mode, camera mode, device class/browser family.
- Server metrics endpoint for Grafana/Prometheus: active rooms, active players, disconnected players, Socket.IO event counters, failed join/reconnect counters, exception counts, report counts, process uptime, optional handler latency buckets.
- Keep Grafana metrics numeric and low-cardinality. Do not label metrics by raw room, player, seed, or error message.
- Use PostHog for rich product correlation and Grafana for infra/service trends. Do not duplicate every gameplay event into Prometheus.
- Ensure the metrics endpoint can be safely scraped by the PPaS/Triton Grafana path, ideally behind network or Cloudflare Access rules from the infra bead.

Acceptance criteria:
- Grafana can graph active rooms/players, disconnect rate, exception rate, process health, and Socket.IO event volume without needing Loki.
- PostHog can correlate low-FPS samples with reports/resets/reconnect failures by release/match/device class.
- Tests cover metric names, low-cardinality labels, and no raw room/player identifiers in metrics.
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Normal unit/server/browser CI: RUM sample and metrics tests cover FPS/frame-time buckets, WebGL init/context loss, visibility/backgrounding, reconnect count, player count, topology/ruleset, camera mode, device/browser class, active rooms/players, disconnects, exceptions, report counts, and Socket.IO event volume.
- Grafana guard: metric names and labels are low-cardinality and do not contain raw room/player/seed/error-message values.
- PostHog guard: RUM samples are low-rate and correlate with reports/resets/reconnect failures by release/match/device class.
- Scrape proof: metrics endpoint behavior is safe for the central Grafana path and documented for local/staging/prod.
close_reason: Closed after independent FreshRUMMetricsValidator PASS and refreshed coordinator checks. Evidence: npx vitest run tests/unit/telemetry-contract.test.js tests/unit/telemetry-client.test.js => 52 passed; python -m unittest server.test_telemetry => 9 passed; validator live-scraped /metrics HTTP 200 with low-cardinality jj_server_* metrics and confirmed RUM FPS/frame-time buckets, visibility, WebGL lifecycle, reconnect, release/match/device context, privacy guards, and scrape docs. Coordinator also fixed the local scrape URL doc nit from :5000 to the server default :8000 before close.
