title: Promote /car-viewer debug editor into the vehicle QA tool
description:
Make the existing `frontend/car-viewer` debug editor a first-class vehicle QA and authoring support tool in the model-import plan. This bead is about preserving and hardening the useful editor that already exists, then wiring it into the evidence flow for per-model work.

Background:
The current `/car-viewer` tool already loads `/debug-cars/manifest.json`, classifies materials into body/wheel/glass/light/other groups, supports recolor modes, shows headlights/tail lights/underglow/name tag/number behavior, and includes rig/suspension preview controls. That is valuable planning infrastructure and should not be treated as a throwaway debug page.

Scope:
- Keep `/car-viewer` or its successor as a documented local QA/editor surface for vehicle imports.
- Ensure it can load both `static/debug-cars/*` sources and normalized selectable vehicle catalog entries, so agents can compare raw imports against production-ready output.
- Preserve controls for material classification, naive-vs-normalized recolor comparison, paint color swatches, wheel/rig preview, suspension bob/steer preview, headlights/tail lights, underglow, name tag, number/decal readability, wireframe, and multi-angle inspection.
- Add or document screenshot/export evidence from the tool: material role summary, texture/material counts, active vehicle id, color, light toggle state, and captured views.
- Make clear that this is a local QA/debug tool, not a player-facing car picker. The actual picker still uses sanitized catalog ids and appearance payloads.
- Use the editor findings to feed the vehicle-model-validation Stage H report rather than replacing the formal gate.

Acceptance criteria:
- The model-import plan names `/car-viewer` (or successor path) as the manual/debug QA tool and requires its evidence for per-model visual review.
- The tool can inspect a raw debug car and a normalized selectable vehicle entry without hand-editing source code.
- Screenshots or exported diagnostics show recolor separation, wheel/rig preview, lights/underglow/name/number state, and material classification results.
- The workflow documents how an agent uses the tool before closing a model bead and how failures become authoring fixes rather than ad-hoc runtime hacks.
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Normal unit CI: `npx vitest run tests/unit/car-viewer-diagnostics.test.js tests/unit/safe-rendering.test.js` covers diagnostics schema, material classification, recolor separation metadata, and literal rendering of hostile model names/errors.
- Normal E2E: `npx playwright test tests/e2e/car-viewer.spec.ts tests/e2e/console-errors.spec.ts` proves raw debug-car and normalized catalog modes load, canvas is nonblank, screenshots/diagnostics hooks work, and no console errors occur.
- Evidence bundle: exported diagnostics include active vehicle id, raw/normalized source, material roles, texture/material counts, paint color, light/underglow/name/number state, and multi-angle screenshots.
- Scope guard: `/car-viewer` is a local QA tool and never a player-facing trust boundary; production picker continues to use sanitized catalog ids.

title: Vehicle material, texture, and light acceptance gate
description:
Add an explicit material/texture/light acceptance gate to the vehicle import pipeline so imported models do not merely load - they are readable, recolorable, lit correctly, and production-usable in the actual game.

Background:
The model-prep plan already requires Stage H validation, but recent work showed unresolved issues around texturing/coloring and vehicle lights. The gate needs to make these failures first-class: no single-color blobs, no baked-light texture problems that block recolor, no missing paint slot, no wheels/glass/tyres recolored accidentally, no embedded GLB lights/cameras, and no headlights/tail lights that point the wrong way or detach from the rig.

Scope:
- Define and enforce normalized material roles: `paint`, `tyre`, `glass`, `metal/trim`, `light`, and any allowed decals/number surfaces.
- Require production recolor behavior: changing player color affects only paint/approved decal surfaces while tyres remain neutral, glass stays readable, lights remain emissive, and numbers/name markers stay legible.
- Validate texture/material budgets: texture sizes, material count, draw-call implications, no surprise giant baked textures, no embedded cameras, no embedded runtime lights, and no per-car PointLights in the selectable GLB path.
- Validate lighting/readability in actual host-style rendering, not only a neutral model viewer: front/rear/side/top screenshots, at least two paint colors, lights on/off, underglow where applicable, and a dark/neon track lighting case.
- Verify headlights/tail lights are oriented correctly, ride with suspension/body transforms, do not float when the rig animates, and remain visible without becoming blinding.
- Feed both deterministic checks and visual review: glTF inspection output, `/car-viewer` diagnostics, headless render screenshots, and the vehicle-model-validation Stage H report.
- Failure mode must be authoring/normalization fixes, not runtime heuristic hacks that hide bad source assets.

Acceptance criteria:
- The prep-manifest runner records material-role mapping, texture/material counts, recolor evidence, and light evidence for each model.
- A model cannot pass if paint recolor also tints tyres/glass/lights, if `paint` resolves to nothing, if light materials are missing/wrongly oriented, or if embedded lights/cameras sneak into selectable vehicles.
- Evidence includes `/car-viewer` diagnostics plus actual host/render-harness screenshots for default color and at least two player colors.
- The vehicle-model-validation Stage H completion note must call out material role pass/fail, texture budget pass/fail, and light orientation/readability pass/fail separately from physics/balance.
- At least the first two imported cars receive manual human playtest review before the broad model rollout continues.
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Normal unit CI: `npx vitest run tests/unit/vehicle-material-gate.test.js tests/unit/vehicle-light-gate.test.js` rejects missing paint, paint bleeding into tyres/glass/lights, embedded cameras/lights, over-budget textures/materials, wrong light orientation, and unresolved material roles.
- Normal E2E/visual QA: `npx playwright test tests/e2e/car-viewer.spec.ts` captures default color plus at least two player colors across front/rear/side/top and a dark/neon lighting preset.
- Stage H report: vehicle-model-validation output must list material role pass/fail, texture budget pass/fail, recolor pass/fail, light orientation/readability pass/fail, and host-style render pass/fail separately from physics/balance.
- Manual gate linkage: first two pilot imports cannot unlock broad rollout until `br-modes-remote-play-design-48a.11.39` records cdilga's playtest approval.

title: Manual human playtest gate after first two vehicle imports
description:
Manual coordinator gate: the first two imported/normalized vehicle models must be personally playtested by cdilga before the broader vehicle rollout continues. Agents must not close this bead based only on automated tests, screenshots, or their own judgment.

Default first-two pair for graph enforcement:
- `br-modes-remote-play-design-48a.11.10` hatchback-sports
- `br-modes-remote-play-design-48a.11.11` sedan-sports

If the human coordinator chooses a different first-two pair before work starts, update this bead dependencies and description to match that explicit choice. Until then, the graph treats those two starter cars as the pilot imports.

Scope of the human playtest:
- Review each of the two cars in the car selection/customization path and the actual host game render.
- Try at least one race and one derby context if both are available at that point, or explicitly record which mode was unavailable.
- Check the subjective parts automation can miss: does the car look good, does recolor preserve readable materials, do lights/readability feel right, does the silhouette match the intended archetype, does the vehicle feel usable rather than merely passing physics metrics.
- Confirm the vehicle-model-validation Stage H evidence includes material/texture/light results, `/car-viewer` evidence, host screenshots, and relevant test/check output.

Acceptance criteria:
- This bead can close only after cdilga has actually playtested the first two cars at that time and posted explicit approval in Beads or Agent Mail.
- The approval must identify the two vehicle ids reviewed and state PASS for broad rollout, or list blockers/fixes.
- If either car fails the human review, this gate stays open and the failed model bead(s) get reopened or receive follow-up beads.
- No remaining per-model import/balance beads should be treated as ready for broad rollout until this manual gate is closed.
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Manual only: cdilga must personally playtest the two pilot vehicle ids at that time; agents cannot close this from automated checks, screenshots, or proxy judgment.
- Required human note in Beads or Agent Mail: identifies the two vehicle ids, confirms race and derby tried or states which mode was unavailable, and says either PASS for broad rollout or lists blockers/fixes.
- Required technical evidence before human review: Stage H reports for both pilot cars include material/texture/light results, `/car-viewer` diagnostics, host/render screenshots, and relevant unit/E2E/check output.
- Failure behavior: if either car fails, this gate stays open and the failed per-model bead receives blockers or follow-up beads; no later per-model rollout bead should be treated as unblocked.

comments.author: OliveFox
comments.text: Human follow-up 2026-06-29: cdilga confirmed this first-two-car playtest gate is intentionally a blocker. Do not bypass it with automation, screenshots, or agent judgment; broad model rollout remains blocked until the human playtest note explicitly passes the pilot cars.

title: Deterministic CV/geometry gates (coverage, symmetry, pivot/orbit, deltaE, ground, number)
description: Hard CI gates from model+renders: AABB vs length, ground contact, wheel perpendicular-offset/no-orbit (seeded by inspect-wheel-pivots.mjs), collider coverage, CoM below center, paint deltaE, left/right symmetry, number legibility, debris count/velocity. docs/plans/per-model-game-readiness-and-balance-2026-06-28.md
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Normal unit CI: deterministic CV/geometry tests cover silhouette coverage, left/right symmetry, wheel pivot/orbit, wheel contact/ground offset, color deltaE for paint variants, roof number position/legibility, collider-fit overlay, and material role masks.
- Regression proof: bad fixtures fail for wheel orbit, missing paint, paint leaking to tyres/glass/lights, wrong ground offset, unreadable number, and asymmetric/cropped render.
- Determinism proof: same model/build/camera preset produces stable measurements within documented tolerances.
- Stage H integration: prep-manifest and vehicle-model-validation consume these gates and record pass/fail separately from physics/balance.

title: Vision-LLM rubric judge + calibration
description: Vendor-agnostic vision-LLM judge over the multi-angle renders returning forced JSON (rubric.template.json). Calibrate against a human-labeled starter-set sample before gating. Layers 1-2 own measurements; this owns 'looks right/on-brand'. docs/plans/per-model-game-readiness-and-balance-2026-06-28.md
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Artifact: a calibrated vision-LLM rubric or equivalent visual review prompt/checklist covers recognizability, material separation, color readability, number readability, wheel rig sanity, light readability, scale, camera views, and obvious visual defects.
- Calibration proof: known-good and known-bad fixtures produce expected pass/fail or human-review-needed verdicts with reasons.
- Evidence guard: LLM rubric verdict cannot replace deterministic gates; it is an additional review layer recorded in Stage H.
- Privacy/repro guard: screenshots sent to any model are generated artifacts only, with no secrets/tokens/user data, and the verdict is saved with prompt/model/version where available.

title: Headless physics sim metrics (top speed, accel, turn radius, settle, rollover, wall-graze)
description: Headless PhysicsSystem loop measuring objective per-model metrics via vc.currentVehicleSpeed() etc. Feeds archetype target bands and the balance gate. docs/plans/per-model-game-readiness-and-balance-2026-06-28.md
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Normal integration CI: headless physics tests measure top speed, acceleration, braking, turn radius, settle time, rollover tendency, wall-graze behavior, bad-landing stability, and reset/recovery behavior for representative vehicles.
- Regression fixtures: wrong scale, too-high center of mass, bad collider fit, excessive rollover, wheel orbit, and wall snag cases fail with diagnostics.
- Evidence artifact: physics metrics JSON includes vehicle id, collider dimensions, mass/CoM, suspension/wheel params, tuning hash, seed, and pass/fail thresholds.
- Stage H integration: prep-manifest and per-model beads consume these metrics before balance or manual playtest claims.

title: Balance framework: stat schema + archetype map + transfer fn + Monte-Carlo win-rate gate
description: Mario-Kart-style net-fair balance: fine-grained int stats (Speed/Accel/Weight/Handling/Traction + hidden boost reusing stunt charge), shared point budget with inverse coupling, silhouette->archetype map, stats->Rapier transfer function, Monte-Carlo win-rate spread gate (winRateTolerance). docs/plans/per-model-game-readiness-and-balance-2026-06-28.md
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Artifact: checked-in stat schema, archetype map, shared point budget, inverse coupling rules, and stats-to-Rapier transfer function define Speed/Accel/Weight/Handling/Traction and any hidden boost/stunt fields.
- Normal unit/integration CI: balance tests cover transfer-function output ranges, archetype bands, deterministic Monte Carlo race/derby simulations, win-rate spread tolerance, and no hidden model advantage outside declared stats.
- Tuning evidence: each starter archetype has a metrics report showing performance, handling, stability, and win-rate spread under the same seed/script suite.
- Gameplay guard: balance changes must not bypass material/visual Stage H, physics stability gates, or the first-two-car human playtest gate.

title: Destructible debris system (detach parts, inherit velocity, TTL, pooling) in DamageSystem
description: Generic detachable-debris on damage:destroyed (both modes): reparent declared debris-* parts, dynamic body + cheap collider, transfer velocity + blast impulse, TTL fade, object pool with cap. Per-model beads only declare which parts shed. docs/plans/per-model-game-readiness-and-balance-2026-06-28.md
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Normal unit/integration CI: debris tests cover per-model debris manifest loading, wheel/panel detach, velocity inheritance, impulse direction, pooling, TTL for this generic system, cleanup, and no NaN/Inf state.
- Visual evidence: render/harness screenshots or captures show detached wheels and panels leaving the wreck, with debris material roles and scale sane.
- Performance guard: body/render counts stay within the generic pool cap for 4/8/16 vehicle destruction stress; no per-frame logging.
- Scope handoff: this bead provides the generic destructible debris source; persistent round-level hazard priority/budget is owned by `br-captain-call-architecture-hardening-woq.6`.

title: Prep-manifest stage runner (model-prep.json, gates A-H)
description: Stage runner that advances a model through A-H, records results in model-prep.json (schema in skill), resumable/auditable. Gates per stage. docs/plans/per-model-game-readiness-and-balance-2026-06-28.md
acceptance_criteria:
The prep-manifest runner is complete only if it makes the model QA gates auditable and hard to skip.

- `model-prep.json` records stage results for normalization, rigging, collider/CoM, balance, debris, color/material QA, light QA, and final Stage H validation.
- The runner stores or links the evidence needed by `br-modes-remote-play-design-48a.11.38`: material role map, texture/material counts, recolor screenshots, `/car-viewer` diagnostics, host/render-harness screenshots, and light orientation/readability checks.
- A model cannot advance to Stage H if material roles are unresolved, paint recolor leaks into tyres/glass/lights, headlights/tail lights fail orientation/attachment/readability checks, or embedded cameras/lights are present in selectable vehicles.
- Output is resumable and clear enough that another agent can inspect why a model is blocked without rerunning every stage.
- The runner does not bypass the manual first-two-car playtest gate; it prepares evidence, while `br-modes-remote-play-design-48a.11.39` controls broad rollout after the first two pilot cars.

title: Remote Play mode: separated players join a shared host-rendered room
description:
New Remote Play mode so people in different locations play together. Builds on the Local single-screen model but lets every participant join from their own computer/device into the same room.

Proposed architecture (host-authoritative):
- One participant is the HOST: runs the authoritative physics sim and the canonical render, exactly like the big screen does today.
- Everyone else gets their OWN browser view of the gameplay (a VIEWER render driven by state synced from the host) PLUS controller input for their car. This sidesteps running N independent authoritative sims and keeps everyone in sync.
- Any number of people can play; non-driving spectators are viewers-only.
- Host migration / 'host leaves' handling: at minimum, detect host loss and end/park the match gracefully; ideally promote another participant.

Open questions to resolve during design (capture decisions in the bead before coding):
- State sync granularity & rate (full snapshots vs deltas) for the viewer render.
- Latency handling for the local player's own car (client-side prediction vs pure mirror).
- Bandwidth ceiling for 'any number' of viewers.

Depends on the game-mode architecture bead. Coordinate with rejoinable-rooms and deeplink-invite.
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Normal server/client CI: tests prove Remote room topology, host authoritative state, viewer bindings, controller bindings, spectators, and room cleanup without breaking Local mode.
- Normal E2E: Playwright multi-context flow creates a Remote room, joins at least two separated participants, proves each gets viewer+controller or spectator behavior as intended, and verifies host authoritative state drives viewers.
- Sync/perf evidence: diagnostics record snapshot/delta cadence, payload size, reconnect behavior, viewer degradation path, and no uncontrolled per-frame event spam.
- Role guard: viewer-only sockets cannot drive, controller sockets drive only their bound seat, and Local controller phones remain HUD-only.
- Open-design closure: state sync granularity, local prediction vs mirror, bandwidth ceiling, and host-loss behavior are documented or split into follow-up beads before close.

title: Rejoinable rooms: rooms persist across matches and survive disconnects
description:
Make game rooms durable so they can be rejoined after a match ends and after a dropped connection, in both Local and Remote modes.

Scope:
- A room outlives a single match: after results, players stay in the room and can start another match without re-creating/re-sharing a code.
- Reconnect: if a player's socket drops, they can rejoin and reclaim their car/seat (by stable player token, not socket id) within a grace window.
- Server holds room + per-player state (name, car, customization, score) keyed by a persistent token, not the transient connection.
- Clean up genuinely abandoned rooms after a timeout.
- Tests: drop a player mid-match and rejoin to same seat; finish a match and start a new one in the same room.

Foundational for deeplink invites (a link should drop you into a live, rejoinable room).
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Normal server CI: room/seat tests cover room persistence across results/rematch, stable player/seat tokens, mid-match disconnect, grace-window reconnect, expired reconnect, host loss, room TTL, and abandoned-room cleanup.
- Normal E2E: Playwright drops a player mid-match and rejoins the same seat; finishes a match and starts a rematch in the same room without re-sharing a code.
- Identity proof: name, color, number, vehicle selection, score/progression, and controller role survive valid reconnect and do not merge with a different device lacking a valid token.
- Security guard: guessed/reused/stale reconnect ids cannot hijack another seat, and stale controller lease input is rejected.

title: Deeplink invites: one-tap shareable link that joins a specific room instantly
description:
Add shareable invite URLs so someone can join a room without manually typing a room code, while preserving the explicit role-intent flow from the join-route bead.

Scope:
- Generate links encoding the room plus enough non-secret context for route entry, for example `/join/<ROOMCODE>` or `?room=CODE&via=invite`; do not encode authority tokens in public copied links.
- Opening a copied/shared link routes into the join chooser from `br-captain-call-architecture-hardening-woq.11` rather than silently deciding the role. The player chooses whether this device is their screen/viewer that will pair a phone controller, controller-only because another screen is visible, or spectator where supported.
- Host/lobby QR remains separate from copied links: scanning it implies the host screen is visible and routes directly to controller-only/HUD-only.
- Pair QR remains separate from join links: scanning it binds a phone controller to an existing viewer/screen seat.
- Provide Copy invite/share affordances in host lobby and, for Remote/mixed where allowed, participant/viewer UI.
- Gracefully handle room ended, invalid/expired code, incompatible topology, revoked/locked access where explicitly supported, and pair-token failures with clear recovery actions.
- Do not treat high occupancy as a routine capacity-limit failure. Copied invites should keep attempting to join/bind in 32/60/100-player situations, then rely on camera/render degradation, ruleset late-join policy, diagnostics, or real technical failure handling if the system cannot keep up.
- Pairs with rejoinable rooms so a link works mid-session, not only at room creation.
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Normal unit/browser CI: invite route tests cover copied invite link, host QR, pair QR, reconnect link, invalid room, expired room, room ended, incompatible topology, high-occupancy/capacity-stress room, and the copied-link role chooser.
- Normal E2E: opening a copied Remote/mixed invite joins the intended room and shows the explicit choice between second-screen/viewer-with-phone-pair, controller-only because the game screen is visible, and spectator where supported.
- Second-screen E2E: choosing the screen/viewer option from a copied invite starts the viewer flow and displays a pair QR/code; scanning that pair QR with a phone binds the phone as controller for the same seat.
- Host QR regression: scanning the host lobby QR does not show the chooser and goes directly to controller-only/HUD-only without initializing the world renderer.
- Capacity-stress regression: high-occupancy invite tests prove the route does not show a planned capacity-limit rejection; it keeps attempting the join/bind path and surfaces best-effort degradation/diagnostics only when pressure is real.
- UX proof: copied invite, host join QR, and pair QR are visually distinct and have clear labels/states; invalid/expired/ended links show recovery actions without blank pages or silent fallback to another room, while high-occupancy links use capacity-stress language rather than arbitrary-limit copy.
- Architecture guard: Local controller paths stay HUD-only; Remote viewer paths initialize the viewer renderer only after explicit intent and degrade gracefully.

title: Align in-game UI with the front-page neon aesthetic
description:
Bring every in-game surface into line with the front-page (landing) aesthetic. Source of truth is the live landing page; the captured tokens and direction live in docs/design-brief.md.

Scope (audit + restyle, surface by surface):
- Phone controller (player.css): join screen, waiting room, in-game HUD, menus, weapon/fire controls -> glows, glass panels, pill buttons, neon palette, focus states.
- Host screen (host.css): lobby, scoreboard, overlays, results.
- Adopt the shared token set (colors, radii, spacing, glows) rather than ad-hoc hex values currently scattered in host.css/player.css.
- Respect prefers-reduced-motion.
- Test: rebuild (npm run build) and visually verify with playwright headed/screenshots; would each screen sit comfortably next to the landing page?

Note: phrased as 'consistent with whatever the front page IS' — re-check against the live landing page, not a frozen snapshot.
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Build check: `npm run build` passes after token/style changes.
- Normal E2E/visual smoke: `npx playwright test tests/e2e/landing-page.spec.ts tests/e2e/join-flow.spec.ts tests/e2e/game-flow.spec.ts tests/e2e/console-errors.spec.ts` proves host/player/lobby/results surfaces load without console errors and remain usable.
- Screenshot evidence: before/after or final screenshots cover phone join/controller/waiting HUD, host lobby, scoreboard/overlays/results, and at least one reduced-motion state.
- Design guard: reuse shared tokens from the live landing page/design brief; avoid ad-hoc scattered colors, broken focus states, unreadable glow, or text overflow on mobile.
close_reason: PASS in comment 54 after durable 48a.5 screenshot evidence filled the final gate; completion note in comment 55.

That host flow was already covered by the validator's E2E run in comment 51 (23 passed, 1 skipped) and by other host smoke evidence. The purpose here was the missing durable visual screenshot evidence, not another gameplay-start proof.

Requesting a fresh validator to confirm the durable evidence set now satisfies the screenshot acceptance gate. Do not self-close.

All 4 from ONE coherent real session (host room UQNY; player EvidenceP1 joined it; host started the race for the HUD). Probe report: all ok:true, pageErrors=[] on every surface, all PNGs non-empty.

DURABLE, RERUNNABLE HARNESS: .ntm/evidence/48a5/capture-surfaces.mjs — drives the REAL app via the genuine host->join->start-game flow (same selectors as tests/e2e/fixtures.ts). It captures real sessions only; if a surface can't be reached it FAILS honestly (no faked DOM).

EXACT COMMANDS (reproduced):

- (server) source ~/.pyenv/versions/multiplayer-racer/bin/activate && FLASK_DEBUG=0 python server/app.py &

LIMITATIONS / NOTES:
- Headless software GL (SwiftShader) is slow; the HUD requires the host to fully boot the race (~3 min under double-context software GL), so the harness uses long timeouts (180s host engine-init, 120s player game-screen). On a GPU machine it is much faster.

title: Own-car identification: make it obvious which car is yours
description:
Players consistently lose track of their own car. Make own-car identity unmistakable on both the host render and (for Remote) each viewer render.

Scope:
- Persistent marker over/around the local player's car (arrow, ring, or floating name tag) that stays readable at distance and in crowds.
- Use the player's chosen color/customization for the marker.
- Brief 'this is you' highlight on (re)spawn and on rejoin.
- Readable in both race and derby modes; works with camera modes.
- Test: with 4 cars, the local player's car is identifiable at a glance in screenshots.
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Normal unit/browser-light CI: `npx vitest run tests/unit/own-car-marker.test.js` or equivalent covers marker selection, color/number/name inputs, distance scaling, and spawn/rejoin highlight timing.
- Normal E2E/visual smoke: `npx playwright test tests/e2e/camera-modes.spec.ts tests/e2e/game-flow.spec.ts` proves a 4-car Local race and derby scene make the controlled car identifiable at a glance without covering HUD/name tags.
- Remote guard: each Remote viewer identifies its own car independently; Local phones remain HUD-only controllers.
- Evidence artifact: screenshots for race, derby, respawn/rejoin highlight, and a crowded/high-N or simulated crowd case.
notes: Release manager blocked: dirty tree with 49 changed files prevents safe scoping of this bead's validated changes. Awaiting coordinator guidance on file ownership across beads or alternative release strategy.
close_reason: Completed and independently validated: NobleEagle reran own-car-marker vitest 6/6, npm build, and Playwright camera/game flow 11 passed/1 skipped; inspected race, derby, chase-focus, respawn, and rejoin screenshots; Local controller-only guard and Remote architecture scope verified.

comments.author: SageBasin
comments.text: Fresh validation BLOCKED by PurpleWaterfall. Unit own-car-marker tests passed 5/5 and npm run build passed, but E2E camera-modes/game-flow passed 9/11 with two blocking failures: chase mode only shows 2/4 markers vs expected >=3, and derby markers overlap HUD/camera controls, violating the no-HUD-covering acceptance criterion. Respawn/rejoin pulses and Local host-only bootstrap look sound. Do not close; fix safe-area/marker rect/culling or test expectation as appropriate, rerun unit/build/E2E, then request fresh validation.

title: Car models: proper 3D vehicle models with variety
description:
Replace placeholder car geometry with proper 3D car models, with enough variety to support per-player distinction and customization.

Scope:
- Source/build a small set of low-poly arcade car models (bundled, not CDN).
- Load via the existing resource/vehicle factory pattern; keep physics chassis decoupled from the visual mesh.
- Performant for multiplayer (instancing / shared geometry where possible).
- Models tint/skin cleanly for customization (see car-customisation bead).
- Test: models render on host and viewer; no perf regression with 4+ cars.
acceptance_criteria: Before closing this bead, use `.claude/skills/vehicle-model-validation/SKILL.md` and include its PASS report in the completion note. Required evidence includes: normalized material separation (`paint` recolors while `tyre` stays neutral), wheel rig/state preview, scale/origin/forward-axis check, per-model collider fit, screenshots/visual evidence from the actual viewer or game path, and relevant asset/test/check output. The model import is not done if the skill reports blockers, even if gameplay mechanics still function.

title: Car customisation: pick color/skin (and model) from the lobby
description:
Let each player customize their car so identity is personal and persistent.

Scope:
- Lobby/waiting-room customization UI (neon-styled): color, and if available model/skin choice; live preview (reuse the existing car-preview).
- Customization travels with the player token so it persists across matches and rejoins (ties into rejoinable-rooms).
- Drives own-car-identification marker color and other players' readability.
- Sensible defaults / auto-assign distinct colors when players don't choose.
- Test: choose a color, start match, confirm the car uses it on host + viewer; rejoin keeps it.
acceptance_criteria:
Before closing this bead, use `.claude/skills/vehicle-model-validation/SKILL.md` for any selectable vehicle preview/model path changed by the customization UI. Required evidence includes catalog-only vehicle IDs, sanitized appearance payload (`vehicleId`, `color`, `number`, `skinId` or current equivalent), screenshots proving recolor/number readability in the picker and host render, and relevant E2E/check output. The selection/customization work is not done if model validation reports blockers for any selectable vehicle.
- Rollout guard: color/number customization may ship before broad model rollout, but selectable vehicle/model choices must stay catalog-only and cannot expose unapproved models until `br-modes-remote-play-design-48a.11.39` records cdilga pilot approval. Vehicle choice must not create hidden handling advantage without balance evidence.

title: Pickup models: proper 3D models for weapon/item pickups
description:
Give weapon/item pickups proper 3D models instead of placeholders, matching the arcade/neon look.

Scope:
- Bundled low-poly models (or stylized glowing primitives) for each pickup type used in derby.
- Glow/float treatment consistent with the neon aesthetic (drop-shadow/emissive).
- Clear at a glance what each pickup is.
- Performant spawn/despawn.
- Test: pickups render and animate on host + viewer; collecting still works.
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Blocking tool gate: `/weapon-lab` from `br-weapon-test-lab-zas` is available for local pickup/weapon visual verification before this bead closes.
- Normal unit/browser-light CI: `npx vitest run tests/unit/pickup-models.test.js tests/unit/weapon-definitions.test.js` proves every pickup type maps to a bundled asset or stylized primitive with no CDN/runtime URL dependency.
- Normal E2E/visual smoke: `npx playwright test tests/e2e/weapon-lab.spec.ts tests/e2e/game-modes.spec.ts` proves pickups render/animate on host/lab, collecting still works, and no console errors occur.
- Performance/readability guard: pickup assets are low-poly/capped/emissive enough for derby crowd stress and remain distinguishable at a glance in the neon style.
- Dependency guard: `/weapon-lab` from `br-weapon-test-lab-zas` is a blocking prerequisite, not an optional relation; pickup art cannot close until the lab can verify pickup visuals and behavior through the production path.
close_reason: Implemented stylized local pickup visuals for all weapon/item pickup types; StormyMill evidence passed focused unit/build and CobaltTiger fresh validation reran unit 8/8, npm build, and weapon-lab plus game-modes Playwright 5/5 from clean server. /weapon-lab prerequisite br-weapon-test-lab-zas is closed.

title: Lo-fi retro design language ("Skip Bin Arcade")
description: Pivot the whole experience to a committed lo-fi PS1-PS2 look per docs/design/. Grimy posterized world, loud players, one camcorder grade across landing->win, readable always. G1 ratified 2026-06-29 (full pivot, keep neon as accent).

title: G1: Ratify lo-fi-retro pivot; supersede neon design-brief
description: Human decision: full pivot vs 3D-only. RATIFIED 2026-06-29: full pivot incl landing, keep a bit of neon as accent. design-brief.md annotated superseded. See docs/design/README.md.
close_reason: Ratified 2026-06-29: full pivot incl landing, keep neon as accent

title: P1.8: (gated) per-material vertex snap + affine warp
description: Subtle wobble on world; cars likely exempt for readability; toggleable. Gated by G2.
close_reason: Completed and independently validated: CobaltTiger PASS-FULL reran 56 focused unit tests, npm run build, and Playwright material-warp e2e; verified live adaptive/manual/reduce-effects policy drives render.setMaterialWarpEnabled, world-only warp has non-fudged diagnostics with vehicle-readable materials exempt, zero-delta off states, no material replacement/logging, and Local /player remains controller/HUD-only.

comments.text: FRESH VALIDATION: PASS-SLICE / BLOCKED-FULL-BEAD (NobleBay; no edits/close). Reran: vitest material-warp+material-conversion 21/21, npm run build PASS, playwright material-warp.spec.ts 1 passed. Slice B honest: warp is an onBeforeCompile shader hook (no new MeshStandardMaterial; ShaderMaterial only pre-existing sky dome), disabled-by-default via skipBinWarpEnabled uniform, world eligible / vehicle-readable exempt, setMaterialWarpEnabled enables only eligible non-exempt + restores clean, worldVertexDeltaMax computed from REAL geometry (0 disabled -> 0.27 enabled -> 0 restored; vehicle-readable active=0), no per-frame logging, host-only API (no player/GameHost). Keep in_progress; do not close on slice alone.

FILE SCOPE (all NEW files -> zero conflict):

Full RenderSystem re-color to the world palette touches a contested dirty file and is a separate migration; out of scope for this reservation/guard bead. This bead's law (NO loud colors in environment) is enforced and regression-guarded.

Proceeding to implement the three NEW files now. Will post READY with test output + artifact path; will not close.

comments.author: cdilga
comments.text:
PLAN FOR IMPLEMENTATION (no-edit): br-skip-bin-arcade-design-language-5k3.12

Scope read: AGENTS.md, README.md, this bead chain, parent design docs, current git status/diffs, and existing RenderSystem/GameHost/UI touchpoints.

1) Requirements framing from docs
- Design contract already requires persistent overhead identification in README/design: everyone can answer “which car is mine?” quickly and reliably, even under fog/dither, and “which one is mine?” is a top-level scorecard item in [01](docs/design/01-what-makes-a-good-game.md) and [03](docs/design/03-experience-flow.md).
- 2.2 in [04](docs/design/04-implementation-plan.md) is explicit: persistent per-car overhead name tag + color marker, and answerable within `<1s` after join/spawn transitions.
- Existing implementation already has VehicleIdentityOverlay + EventBus bootstrap + math module, but it is not currently guaranteed by current in-progress RenderSystem/GameHost/UI edits (rendered markers are still created by legacy paths too).

2) Conflict-aware plan and likely file scope
- Must not edit while overlap is active:
  - `static/js/GameHost.js`
  - `static/js/systems/RenderSystem.js`
  - `static/js/ui/*` files currently actively modified by other work:
    - `static/js/ui/LobbyUI.js`, `static/js/ui/RoomCodeOverlayUI.js`
  - active active material/render lanes likely to alter marker visuals by overlap:
    - `static/js/resources/VehicleFactory.js`, `static/js/shaders/ColorGradingShader.js`, `static/js/resources/MaterialFactory.js` (if/when present), `static/js/resources/TrackFactory.js`.
- Also defer until coordinator releases RenderSystem/GameHost/UI overlap:
  - `static/js/systems/RenderSystem.js` (overlay lifecycle/legacy tag path + camera mode info/diagnostics)
  - `static/js/GameHost.js` (viewer-player id handoff and marker ownership timing)
  - any direct UI stylesheet/layout touches in `static/css/host.css` that affect marker legibility.
- Proposed final scope once lock is released:
  - Activate/finalize existing identity overlay integration only (no new stack):
    - `static/js/ui/VehicleIdentityOverlay.js`
    - `static/js/ui/vehicleIdentityMath.js`
    - `static/js/ui/VehicleIdentityOverlayBootstrap.js`
  - Validation/contract support:
    - `tests/unit/own-car-marker.test.js`
    - `tests/e2e/game-flow.spec.ts`
    - `tests/e2e/camera-modes.spec.ts`
    - optional: `tests/e2e/visual-effects.spec.ts` extension for grade/dither overlap checks

3) How to make every car have persistent overhead name/tag + marker
- Keep markers model-driven and event-driven in one owner:
  - rely on `Vehicle` entities and `eventBus` events (`network:playerJoined`, `network:playerLeft`, `damage:respawn`, `game:countdown`, etc.).
  - render each car even when camera/depth would normally cull in legacy path.
  - strip legacy per-car `.player-name-tag` usage from active render path (or de-duplicate/disable it) so only one overlay system owns rendering.
  - use `vehicleIdentityMath.js` for deterministic identity rendering rules:
    - `buildMarkerIdentity`
    - `computeMarkerPresentation`
    - `computeMarkerPriority`
    - `estimateMarkerRect` + `rectsOverlap` for overlap arbitration.
  - set viewer-owned marker explicitly when viewer seat is known:
    - `setViewerPlayerId(playerId)` and `isViewerOwned` class/path used for “YOU” badge and stronger emphasis.

4) Stable dimensions and no overlap / crowding strategy
- Hard floor for readability:
  - marker `scale` floor/ceil and opacity floor in `computeMarkerPresentation`.
  - safe-area clamp from `resolveSafeArea` + `clampMarkerPosition` using HUD occluders.
  - overlap arbitration with priority (viewer-owned > preferred/focus > pulsing > distance), hide lower-priority when overlap persists.
- Define acceptance guardrails:
  - marker visible count == player count after active-state settle (or within 1s after join/respawn).
  - marker boxes must not materially cover `#camera-controls`, `#race-timer`, `.hud-lap`, `.hud-health-bars`, `.hud-speed`.
  - marker geometry remains readable under low-res/posterize/fog path by clamping font/box dimensions and avoiding tiny distances-only shrink.

5) which-is-mine under 1s (desktop + mobile path)
- Add a deterministic readiness assertion around overlay state:
  - on each player join/respawn, `window.__vehicleIdentityOverlay.getDebugSnapshot()` should show markerCount >= player count quickly and marker for rejoined seat marked as pulsing within 1s.
  - in e2e, assert `visibility` + `pulsing` transitions after rejoin/respawn with `expect.poll(...).toBe(true)` and `toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1)` timing windows.
- For mobile/desktop evidence:
  - desktop: race + derby 4-car snapshots at 1920x1080 (marker persistence, no HUD overlap, preferred marker remains visible).
  - mobile/viewport evidence: run equivalent assertion on compact host viewport or remote mobile viewer profile, then capture marker-size/readability screenshots (single source for mobile readability).

6) Tests and artifact evidence plan
- Unit:
  - keep/extend `tests/unit/own-car-marker.test.js` for geometry, priority, clamp floors, and non-overlap math behavior.
  - any new contract for overlay visibility timing remains in test helpers.
- E2E:
  - `tests/e2e/game-flow.spec.ts`: extend existing marker tests with:
    - marker appears within 1s after join/respawn/rejoin, and persists across 4-car derby.
    - overlay does not occlude HUD blocks.
  - `tests/e2e/camera-modes.spec.ts`: keep preferred marker identity stable across camera mode switches.
  - optionally add a dedicated `markers-readability.spec.ts` if we want isolated 1s SLA checks.
  - capture screenshots under both normal and degraded grade states.
- Run list (post-lock):
  - `npx vitest run tests/unit/own-car-marker.test.js`
  - `npx playwright test tests/e2e/game-flow.spec.ts --grep "host markers"`
  - `npx playwright test tests/e2e/camera-modes.spec.ts`
  - `npm run build`

7) Dirty/WIP files to avoid touching now
- Do not modify/overwrite unrelated active work tracked in current status:
  - render/analytics/telemetry overlap: `static/js/GameHost.js`, `static/js/systems/RenderSystem.js`
  - UI overlap: `static/js/ui/LobbyUI.js`, `static/js/ui/RoomCodeOverlayUI.js`
  - active style/material pipeline files under current diff/contested scope.

8) Suggested release gate sequencing
- Wait for coordinator approval of overlap release for RenderSystem/GameHost/UI.
- Implement identity overlay hardening + tests only in non-contended files first.
- Then do a focused pass in RenderSystem/GameHost for integration and legacy tag retirement.
- Finalize with e2e visual evidence and marker SLA evidence.

comments.author: StormyBeaver
comments.text:
# br-skip-bin-arcade-design-language-5k3.12 Evidence

Fresh validation by StormyBeaver on 2026-07-02.

## Scope

`5k3.12` requires persistent per-car overhead name tags/color markers so players can identify their
car quickly under the active Skip Bin Arcade grade. The current implementation comes from the
host-only `VehicleIdentityOverlay` path and related marker tests.

## Commands

- `npx vitest run tests/unit/own-car-marker.test.js`
  - Result: pass, 1 file, 6 tests.
- `npm run build`
  - Result: pass. Vite built `dist/` from the current JS/CSS tree. Existing warnings only:
    Vite CJS Node API deprecation, non-module `audioManager.js`, large chunk warning.
- `npx playwright test tests/e2e/game-flow.spec.ts -g "host markers" --workers=1`
  - Result: pass, 2 tests.
  - Covers 4-car race markers, respawn pulse, rejoin pulse, and 4-car derby marker/HUD overlap.
- `npx playwright test tests/e2e/camera-modes.spec.ts --workers=1`
  - Result: pass, 1 test.
  - Covers marker persistence/preferred identity across party/chase/hood camera modes.

## Visual Artifacts

- `own-car-race-4p.png`
  - Four-player race under active grade. The debug assertion proved 4 markers exist and at least 3
    are visible; screenshot shows persistent overhead labels in the race scene.
- `own-car-respawn-pulse.png`
  - Respawn path after forced damage. The Playwright assertion proved a marker entered pulsing state.
    Screenshot framing shows the active grade and visible overhead labels; not every marker is in
    frame.
- `own-car-rejoin-pulse.png`
  - Rejoin path for player 1. The Playwright assertion proved player 1's marker entered pulsing
    state after reconnect.
- `own-car-derby-4p.png`
  - Four-player derby. Shows four overhead labels inside the arena and away from host HUD/camera
    controls; Playwright also checked marker boxes against timer, lap/HUD, speed, and camera-control
    occluder boxes.
- `own-car-chase-focus.png`
  - Chase camera. Shows the focused car marker plus peer markers, all readable with the active
    camera controls visible.

All PNGs were copied immediately after their producing Playwright run because Playwright clears
`test-results/` between runs.

## Coordination Notes

Agent Mail MCP was not reachable and the local `am` CLI was blocked by the running Agent Mail
daemon's SQLite lock during this validation. A direct read-only SQLite check showed zero unexpired
active file reservations before writing this artifact directory. Coordination/closure notes are
mirrored through Beads comments as the documented fallback for this transient Agent Mail outage.

## Residual Risk

The race screenshot has crowded labels because the camera starts with four cars close together, but
the assertions prove marker count and visibility. Higher-order styling such as leader/danger marker
treatment remains in dependent beads (`5k3.14`, `5k3.19`, `5k3.21`, `5k3.26`).

comments.author: ClaudeHealthBars
comments.text:
PRE-EDIT PLAN — ClaudeHealthBars (no-spawn; via br). Scope stays narrow.

CURRENT STATE (why it fails the bead): static/js/ui/RaceUI.js health bars are thin 16px rounded bars with linear-gradient fills (.health-bar-fill.health-high/medium/low use linear-gradient), 11-12px text, border-radius 8px, and a width-animated continuous fill. Thin + gradient + rounded => illegible at couch distance and smears under posterize/dither. Design 02 (flat/matte, hard 1-2px borders, no gradients) + design 03 line 107 ('health as chunky segmented bars not thin') require a segmented redesign.

FILE SCOPE (only these; NOT touching GameHost/RenderSystem/telemetry/MaterialFactory/WeaponSystem/LobbyUI/RoomCodeOverlayUI):
- EDIT static/js/ui/RaceUI.js — health-bar CSS + updateHealthBars() + updatePlayerHealth(); add pure static helpers.
- NEW tests/unit/race-health-bars.test.js — pure segmentation-logic unit tests.
- NEW tests/e2e/health-bars.spec.ts — DOM/visual/stability/no-gradient diagnostic + screenshot artifact.
- NEW artifacts/br-skip-bin-arcade-design-language-5k3.13/ — screenshot + diagnostic json.

DESIGN:
- 10 fixed discrete segments per player bar. segmentsLit = health<=0 ? 0 : clamp(round(pct/10),1,10) (>=1 while alive).
- Flat SOLID loud-palette fills, no gradients: high>50% = #5CFF6A (LOUD.P5), med 25-50% = #FFD23E (LOUD.P3), low<=25% = #FF3B3B (LOUD.P4). Unlit segment = ink; 2px ink gaps + hard 2px ink border, border-radius 0 => survives posterize/dither.
- Couch legibility: taller segments (~26px), bold uppercase ~16px name with dark outline.
- STABLE dimensions: fixed-width track, constant 10 segment DOM nodes regardless of health (only is-lit class + container tier var toggle) — no width-transition reflow. Diagnostic asserts segment count is constant across health values.
- NO overlap: keep .hud-health-bars container class + #health-bars-container id (game-flow.spec.ts + own-car overlap E2E depend on them), keep top:80px/left:20px anchor, add bounded max-width so the panel footprint stays clear of hud-top and name-tag markers. The existing game-flow.spec.ts already asserts vehicle-id markers don't overlap .hud-health-bars — I will run it as the overlap regression guard.
- Data attrs for diagnostics: data-player-id, data-health-percent, data-segments-lit, data-tier.

EVIDENCE I WILL PRODUCE: unit tests (segmentsLit/tier/stability + source-scan that health fills use no linear-gradient); npm run build; focused Playwright spec rendering window.RaceUI health bars at 100/70/40/15/0%, asserting exactly 10 segments each, constant count across values, computed background-image 'none' (solid, not gradient) on lit segments, and panel within viewport; plus a screenshot artifact; and re-run game-flow overlap assertion. READY only after these pass. Not closing. Proceeding to edit RaceUI.js now.

comments.author: ClaudeHealthBars
comments.text:
READY FOR FRESH VALIDATION — ClaudeHealthBars (no-spawn; via br). Not closing.

SCOPE (as planned; no forbidden files touched by THIS task):
- EDIT static/js/ui/RaceUI.js only (137 insertions / 53 deletions).
- NEW tests/unit/race-health-bars.test.js, tests/e2e/health-bars.spec.ts, artifacts/br-skip-bin-arcade-design-language-5k3.13/.
- Disclosure: MaterialFactory.js + WeaponSystem.js also show as dirty, but those are my PRIOR ClaudeMaterialRepair changes for bead 5k3.5 — untouched by this health-bar work. All other dirty files are other agents' pre-existing work, preserved.

WHAT CHANGED: replaced the thin 16px rounded linear-gradient health fill with chunky SEGMENTED bars — 10 fixed discrete segments, flat SOLID loud-palette colors, hard ink borders + 2px inter-segment gaps, border-radius 0, bold uppercase monospace names with hard outline. grep 'linear-gradient|health-bar-fill' in RaceUI.js = 0.

EVIDENCE:
1) Unit — npx vitest run tests/unit/race-health-bars.test.js => 10/10 PASS. Covers pure segmentation (segmentsLit 0@dead, 10@full, >=1 while alive, discrete mapping, never exceeds count), tier bands, and a source-scan proving no gradient fill + solid loud hexes (#5CFF6A/#FFD23E/#FF3B3B).
2) E2E diagnostic — npx playwright test tests/e2e/health-bars.spec.ts => 1/1 PASS. Renders window.RaceUI (isolated container, host HUD untouched) at 100/70/40/15/0%. Asserts: exactly 10 chunky segments each; lit counts 10/7/4/2/0 match data-segments-lit; lit segments computed background-image = 'none' (FLAT SOLID, survives posterize/dither); track height 26px (couch-legible); STABLE dimensions — segment-track width identical (130px) across ALL health values (only lit state toggles, no reflow); panel within viewport (left20/right320<=1280) and clear of the top HUD row (top96 >= hudTop.bottom82). Artifacts written: artifacts/br-skip-bin-arcade-design-language-5k3.13/health-bars.png (visual: green/yellow/red segmented bars) + health-bars-diagnostic.json (machine-readable, loud rgb(92,255,106)/(255,210,62)/(255,59,59) confirmed).
3) No-overlap regression guard — npx playwright test game-flow.spec.ts -g 'host markers stay visible in 4-car derby without covering HUD' => PASS. Confirms vehicle-id name-tag markers still do NOT overlap .hud-health-bars after enlarging the bars (panel container class/id preserved; moved top 80->96 to clear the timer/lap row that the old bars overlapped by 2px).
4) Build — npm run build => vite ✓ built. Full unit suite: 555 passed / 19 failed; the 19 are pre-existing failures in 4 UNRELATED files (controller-input-routing, palette-guard, race-finish-grace, renderer-backend) — identical count before my change; my work adds 10 passing, ZERO new regressions.

MEETS BEAD: chunky segmented (not thin gradient) [design 03 L107]; couch-legible (26px chunks, 16px bold names); dither/posterize-safe (flat solid loud colors + hard borders, no gradients); stable responsive dims (constant 130px track, constant 10-node geometry); no HUD/name-tag overlap (guard + layout assertions). Leaving IN_PROGRESS for fresh validation.

comments.author: FreshHealthBarsValidator
comments.text:
VALIDATION PASS — FreshHealthBarsValidator (independent; no edits/no close/no spawn; Agent Mail down, coordinating via br).

Acceptance under test: "Health legible at host-screen distance; not thin gradients; survives dither" — chunky segmented bars. Inspected ONLY the health-bar scope (static/js/ui/RaceUI.js, tests/unit/race-health-bars.test.js, tests/e2e/health-bars.spec.ts, artifacts/br-skip-bin-arcade-design-language-5k3.13/). Reran focused checks at ~2026-07-01 22:05-22:07 UTC.

FOCUSED CHECKS — all pass:
- npx vitest run tests/unit/race-health-bars.test.js => 10/10 passed.
- npx playwright test tests/e2e/health-bars.spec.ts => 1/1 passed.
- npx playwright test game-flow.spec.ts -g "host markers stay visible" => passed (HUD/name-tag no-overlap regression guard).
- npm run build => vite ✓ built in 2.67s (RaceUI.js was edited after ClaudeHealthBars' build; re-confirmed clean).
- grep -nE "linear-gradient|radial-gradient|health-bar-fill|health-high|health-medium|health-low" static/js/ui/RaceUI.js => 0 matches.
- Visual review of artifacts/.../health-bars.png (see below).

ACCEPTANCE-BY-DIMENSION EVIDENCE:
1) NO gradients remain — CONFIRMED. Diff deletes all three .health-bar-fill.health-{high,medium,low} linear-gradient rules; new .health-seg.is-lit uses `background: var(--health-color)` (flat solid). grep finds zero gradients/old fill classes. E2E asserts every lit segment's computed backgroundImage === 'none'. Diagnostic JSON: litBackgroundImage "none" on all lit bars.
2) EXACTLY 10 STABLE segments — CONFIRMED. RaceUI.SEGMENT_COUNT=10; _segmentTrackHTML() always emits 10 constant .health-seg nodes; per-health change toggles only .is-lit (no node add/remove, no width transition — transition is `background 0.12s steps(1)`). E2E asserts segTotal===10 for all bars and trackWidth identical across ALL health values (Set size===1). Diagnostic: trackWidth 130px constant at 100/70/40/15/0%. Lit counts 10/7/4/2/0 match segmentsLit() discrete mapping and data-segments-lit attr.
3) FLAT LOUD colors — CONFIRMED. tier-high #5CFF6A / tier-medium #FFD23E / tier-low #FF3B3B (reserved loud palette), unlit #2A2620 asphalt, hard ink borders #14110F, border-radius 0. Diagnostic rgb: (92,255,106)/(255,210,62)/(255,59,59). Screenshot shows solid green/yellow/red chunks, no blends.
4) COUCH + DITHER legibility — CONFIRMED. 26px segment track (E2E asserts >=20), 16px bold UPPERCASE monospace names with 4-way hard #14110F text-shadow outline, hard borders + zero rounding + solid fills band cleanly under posterize/dither. Screenshot legible at a glance.
5) ARTIFACTS present + fresh — CONFIRMED. health-bars.png (5 rows PINK/CYAN/YELL/RED/DEAD with 10/7/4/2/0 lit segments) + health-bars-diagnostic.json (machine-readable, panel geometry, per-bar tier/width/color) both regenerated by my E2E run.
6) NO HUD/name-tag overlap — CONFIRMED. Container class .hud-health-bars + id #health-bars-container preserved (game-flow.spec + others depend on them); top moved 80->96 to clear the top HUD row; fixed width 300px + max-width 32vw bounds the footprint. E2E asserts panel within viewport and panel.top(96) >= hudTop.bottom(82). game-flow overlap guard (vehicle-id markers vs .hud-health-bars) passes.

TESTS ARE MEANINGFUL (not vacuous):
- Unit: exercises pure segmentsLit()/healthTier()/healthPercent() incl. edge cases (0=dead->0 lit, alive->>=1 floor, 45->5, 25->3 discrete rounding, never exceeds 10, clamp, maxHealth<=0) and tier band boundaries (50->medium, 51->high, 25->low, 26->medium); source-scan proves segmented markup present + fill classes absent + loud hexes present.
- E2E: invokes the REAL RaceUI (new RaceUI(...).updateHealthBars(players)) and asserts live DOM/computed-style/getBoundingClientRect geometry — genuine, not stubbed.

RESIDUAL RISKS (all non-blocking):
- E2E renders RaceUI into an isolated container (not the live host HUD path) to avoid mutating the host HUD; it still executes the real updateHealthBars/_applyHealth/_segmentTrackHTML code, and HUD overlap is separately covered by the geometry assertion + the game-flow guard. Acceptable isolation choice.
- Unit source-scan's gradient assertion is narrow (`linear-gradient(...#00ff88)`), but backed by `not.toMatch(/health-bar-fill/)` and my independent repo grep (0 linear-gradient in RaceUI.js), so gradient-freedom is genuinely established.
- Scope hygiene: RaceUI.js is the only production file changed for this bead; MaterialFactory.js/WeaponSystem.js remain dirty from the separately-validated 5k3.5 work (disclosed by author), untouched here. No forbidden files (GameHost/RenderSystem/telemetry/LobbyUI/RoomCodeOverlayUI) modified.

Conclusion: chunky segmented (10 fixed slots, not thin gradient), flat loud solid colors, stable 130px/26px geometry, couch+dither legible, artifacts fresh, and no HUD/name-tag overlap — all independently reproduced. VALIDATION PASS. Leaving the actual close to owner/coordinator per no-close constraint.

comments.author: cdilga
comments.text:
# br-skip-bin-arcade-design-language-5k3.14 — P2.4 Leader marker + danger styling

**Agent:** CobaltTiger · **Status:** READY for fresh validation (NOT closed) · Date: 2026-07-02

## Acceptance → proof

| Acceptance | Implementation | Proof |

comments.author: ClaudeHitStopPlan
comments.text:
CONFLICT-AWARE PLAN (NO EDITS) — ClaudeHitStopPlan. Smash hit-stop, 1-3 frame sub-perceptual freeze. Read AGENTS.md/README/design-02+03/this bead + dirty diffs. Deps 5k3.13 (health bars) + 5k3.5 (materials) are done.

== ARCHITECTURE CONSTRAINT (drives everything) ==
GameLoop runs physics on a FIXED deterministic timestep (loop:update dt=1/60) with replay/determinism (engine/determinism.js, replayJournal.js). Therefore hit-stop MUST be a RENDER/PRESENTATION effect, NEVER a physics time-scale. No global slow-mo: on a shared host screen freezing the whole sim would freeze uninvolved racers and break determinism/replay. Bead says the same: 'NOT global slow-mo on shared screen - mid-race uses localized camera punch-in.'

== TIMING MODEL (non-global, host-safe, deterministic) ==
- Frame-based counter (1-3 render frames, ~16-50ms), decremented on eventBus 'loop:render'. NOT a physics timeScale; loop:update untouched => determinism/replay preserved.
- Severity gate = 'weight not lag' sweet spot: only trigger above a heavy threshold; map severity->frames (light=1, med=2, heavy/elimination=3), hard cap 3. Single active hit-stop; re-trigger refreshes but never exceeds cap (anti-lag). Upstream 500ms collision cooldown (DamageSystem) already debounces => no spam.
- Two effect MODES chosen by CONTEXT policy:
  (A) LOCALIZED VISUAL FREEZE — for focused/elimination beats (damage:destroyed, derby, or a viewport tile focused on the pair): hold ONLY the involved vehicles' mesh sync (skip mesh<-body copy) for N render frames; physics keeps stepping; interpolation smooths catch-up. Non-global: other cars keep moving.
  (B) CAMERA PUNCH-IN — for shared mid-race with multiple active racers: NO freeze; a 2-4 frame directional zoom/kick toward the impact, then ease back. This is the bead's explicit mid-race path.

== TRIGGERS (already emitted — NO edits to emitters) ==
DamageSystem emits: damage:vehicleCollision {vehicleA,vehicleB,damage}, damage:destroyed {vehicleId,playerId}, damage:barrierCollision {vehicleId,damage,speed}. WeaponSystem emits: weapon:hit, weapon:explosion. 'damage' is the severity signal (relative-speed derived). RenderSystem ALREADY subscribes to damage:vehicleCollision (strength=damage/40), weapon:explosion, damage:destroyed and calls addImpactShake (RenderSystem.js:645-657) with existing this.cameraShake infra (RenderSystem.js:218). => The camera punch-in is a natural extension of that existing shake path (also the sibling of dependent 5k3.16 screen-shake). I will SUBSCRIBE to these events; emitters (DamageSystem/WeaponSystem) need zero changes.

== FILE SCOPE ==
NO-CONFLICT, buildable independently (new files; DamageSystem/WeaponSystem subscribe-only):
- NEW static/js/systems/HitStopController.js — PURE logic (no THREE/DOM/RenderSystem): input {severity, context} -> decision {mode:'freeze'|'camera-punch'|'none', frames 1-3, intensity}; tick(frame) countdown; cap/refresh; deterministic. 100% unit-testable now.
- NEW static/js/systems/HitStopSystem.js — thin wiring: eventBus subscriptions + loop:render tick + calls the RenderSystem hooks below.
- NEW tests/unit/hit-stop.test.js — severity->frames, threshold gating (light hits ignored), 3->2->1->0 countdown, cap@3, context policy (shared-race=>camera-punch, focused=>freeze), no-stack/refresh, determinism (same inputs=>same schedule). Synthetic frame ticks, no DOM.
- NEW tests/e2e/hit-stop.spec.ts — drive a heavy collision; assert involved mesh world-pos HOLDS for N frames while physics body advances (freeze mode) or camera punch offset applied (mid-race); screenshot artifact; assert no per-tick log spam (reuse console-errors.spec.ts:127 pattern).

MUST-WAIT integration edits (2 small hooks in HOT files — do NOT touch until coordinator releases the render/host overlap):
1) static/js/systems/RenderSystem.js — MUST WAIT. Actively edited (+156 dirty) and on the render wait-list. Needs: (a) extend the existing addImpactShake/cameraShake into a directional 'camera punch-in' applied in _updateCamera/_applyCameraShake (RenderSystem.js:948/995); (b) honor a per-vehicle 'visual-hold-until-frame' flag in the mesh<-body sync so mode A can freeze only the involved cars. Both are additive hooks; conflict risk is high because render owner is mid-edit on this exact camera/mesh path.
2) static/js/GameHost.js — MUST WAIT. Actively edited by analytics rkt.7 (+474 dirty). Needs ~2 lines to construct/register HitStopSystem next to the other systems (GameHost.js:412-420) and pass render+eventBus refs.
COORDINATE (dirty, maybe unneeded): static/js/engine/Engine.js (+32) only if HitStopSystem registers via Engine.registerSystem / needs runContext; prefer wiring through GameHost to avoid Engine.
DO NOT TOUCH: static/js/player.js (+204, rkt.7) — host-side effect, not needed. Telemetry files (TelemetryClient/Service/index) — not needed; if a bounded 'feel:hitStop' analytics event is later wanted it overlaps rkt.7/rkt.8 taxonomy => defer + coordinate, do not edit.

== TUNING / DEBUG EVIDENCE ==
- Tuneable constants on HitStopController (heavyThreshold, frames-by-severity, punchIn intensity/frames, easing) with setters mirroring DamageSystem/cameraShake patterns; candidate to externalize to JSON per data-over-code principle.
- One-time debug line per event (severity, mode, frames) behind a host/URL debug flag — NEVER per-frame (CLAUDE.md + console-errors guard).

== SEQUENCING (lets ~80% proceed without conflict) ==
Phase 1 (no conflict, on release-to-implement): HitStopController + hit-stop.test.js — full decision/timing logic proven in isolation.
Phase 2 (needs coordinator release of RenderSystem + GameHost): add the 2 render hooks + 1 registration line + HitStopSystem wiring + e2e/visual + build.
I will re-diff RenderSystem/GameHost right before Phase 2 to preserve active WIP.

Blocking asks to coordinator: (1) confirm the render/mesh/camera hook approach in RenderSystem and when the render owner releases; (2) a short GameHost registration window from rkt.7; (3) confirm no 'feel:*' telemetry is expected from this bead (keep it out of rkt.7/rkt.8's lane). No files modified. Not closing.

comments.author: ClaudeHitStopPlan
comments.text:
PHASE 1 READY — ClaudeHitStopPlan. Deterministic hit-stop core implemented per coordinator's Phase-1 release. Not closing; Phase 2 render/host integration remains held.

FILES (exactly the allowed scope; nothing else touched):
- NEW static/js/systems/HitStopController.js — pure decision/timing core (no THREE/DOM/EventBus/renderer).
- NEW tests/unit/hit-stop.test.js — 26 tests.
- NEW artifacts/br-skip-bin-arcade-design-language-5k3.15/hit-stop-decision-matrix.json — tuning evidence.
Confirmed via git status: only HitStopController.js, hit-stop.test.js, artifacts/ are new. RenderSystem.js/GameHost.js/Engine.js/DamageSystem.js/WeaponSystem.js/player.js/telemetry/E2E: NOT modified this phase (their dirty diffs are other agents'/prior-bead WIP, preserved).

WHAT IT DOES (maps to acceptance):
- Severity threshold 'weight not lag': severity<heavyThreshold(0.25) => mode 'none', 0 frames (light taps ignored).
- 1-3 frame cap: severity bands ->1/2/3 frames; hard cap maxFrames=3; classify() clamps even if config asks more (test: eliminationFrames:10 => 3).
- Refresh, no-stack (anti-lag): repeat impacts refresh window to max(remaining,new) capped at 3, never sum; 20 rapid elimination impacts => framesRemaining<=3; weaker impact never shortens a stronger ongoing effect; stronger impact upgrades mode+intensity mid-effect.
- Context policy (no global slow-mo): 'focused' => 'freeze' (freezesMeshes true); 'shared-race' => 'camera-punch' (freezesMeshes false); unknown/missing context defaults to shared-race (safe, no freeze); a mid-race elimination stays 'camera-punch' — never a global freeze.
- Fixed-physics safety: physicsTimeScale getter is ALWAYS 1 (verified before/after register + tick + in state snapshot); controller exposes no time-scale mutation. Freeze is render-only (mesh-hold flag), so the deterministic fixed-timestep sim/replay is never touched.
- Tunable constants: DEFAULT_CONFIG {heavyThreshold, band1, band2, maxFrames, eliminationFrames, eliminationMinIntensity}; constructor overrides + setConfig() runtime setters (tests cover both).
- Determinism: identical call sequences produce byte-identical state snapshots (JSON.stringify compared step-by-step across two instances).
- No per-tick logging: tick() never writes console; spy test asserts 0 console.log/warn/error across register + 200 ticks; lastImpact (debug read) is set only on registerImpact, never mutated by tick().

TEST EVIDENCE — npx vitest run tests/unit/hit-stop.test.js:
  RUN v3.2.4
  ✓ tests/unit/hit-stop.test.js (26 tests) 6ms
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests  26 passed (26)

Full unit suite — npx vitest run: Test Files 4 failed | 47 passed (51); Tests 19 failed | 584 passed (603). My 26 hit-stop tests all pass; the 19 failures are PRE-EXISTING and confined to 4 UNRELATED files (controller-input-routing, palette-guard, race-finish-grace, renderer-backend) — identical failure count before this phase => ZERO new regressions from Phase 1.

ARTIFACT: hit-stop-decision-matrix.json holds the config, an 8-row input->decision matrix (e.g. 0.10/focused=>none; 0.25/focused=>freeze/1; 0.90/focused=>freeze/3; 0.90/shared-race=>camera-punch/3; 0.05/shared-race+elim=>camera-punch/3 intensity>=0.8), and a 3-frame countdown demo showing framesRemaining 3->2->1->0 with physicsTimeScale=1 throughout.

Phase 2 (HELD): the 2 RenderSystem hooks (camera-punch via existing addImpactShake/cameraShake path RenderSystem.js:645-657/995; per-vehicle mesh-hold flag honoring freezesMeshes) + 1 GameHost registration line + HitStopSystem wiring + e2e/visual proof — pending coordinator release of the render/host overlap. Not closing the bead.

comments.author: FreshHitStopCoreValidator
comments.text:
PHASE 1 VALIDATION PASS — FreshHitStopCoreValidator (independent; no edits/no close/no spawn; Agent Mail down, coordinating via br).

Validated ClaudeHitStopPlan PHASE 1 READY (comment @12:23): deterministic hit-stop decision/timing core. Reran the required check + inspected source, tests, artifact, and scope at ~2026-07-01 22:24 UTC.

REQUIRED CHECK (reran):
- npx vitest run tests/unit/hit-stop.test.js => tests/unit/hit-stop.test.js (26) — 26 passed (1 file). Matches READY.

SCOPE COMPLIANCE — CONFIRMED:
- git status: static/js/systems/HitStopController.js, tests/unit/hit-stop.test.js, artifacts/br-skip-bin-arcade-design-language-5k3.15/ are NEW/untracked (??). No tracked production file was modified by this phase.
- Guarded files RenderSystem.js, GameHost.js, engine/Engine.js, DamageSystem.js, WeaponSystem.js, player.js all show as dirty (M) but that is prior/other-bead WIP (analytics rkt.7 + render work), NOT this phase — confirmed by grep: `grep -rniE "hitstop|hit-stop|hit_stop"` over RenderSystem/GameHost/Engine/DamageSystem/WeaponSystem/player/telemetry/e2e => ZERO references. Phase 1 did not wire the controller into any guarded file (correct: Phase 2 integration is held).
- HitStopController.js is pure: no import of THREE/DOM/EventBus/renderer (verified by reading the full file); 100% unit-testable in isolation.

ACCEPTANCE-BY-DIMENSION (source + tests):
1) Deterministic timing — CONFIRMED. Frame-based counters only; no Date.now/performance.now/Math.random anywhere. Test "identical call sequences produce identical state snapshots" compares JSON.stringify(state) byte-for-byte across two instances step-by-step.
2) 1-3 frame cap — CONFIRMED. severityToFrames bands: <0.25=>0, [0.25,0.55)=>1, [0.55,0.80)=>2, >=0.80=>3 (HitStopController.js:107-113); classify() clamps `Math.max(0, Math.min(maxFrames=3, frames))` (L132). Test proves eliminationFrames:10 config still yields frames=3 and framesRemaining<=3.
3) Refresh / no-stack (anti-lag) — CONFIRMED. registerImpact when active: `refreshed = min(maxFrames, max(framesRemaining, decision.frames))` — takes the longer, never sums, capped (L180-183). Tests: repeat 3f after 1 tick => 3 (not 5); weaker impact doesn't shorten stronger; 20 rapid eliminations => framesRemaining<=3; stronger impact upgrades mode+intensity mid-effect.
4) Context policy (no global slow-mo) — CONFIRMED. normalizeContext defaults unknown/missing => 'shared-race' (safe, L96-98); mode = context==='focused' ? freeze : camera-punch, governed by CONTEXT not elimination (L138-141). Tests: focused=>freeze(freezesMeshes true), shared-race=>camera-punch(freezesMeshes false), missing/'weird'=>camera-punch, mid-race elimination=>camera-punch (never a global freeze).
5) physicsTimeScale fixed at 1 — CONFIRMED. Getter returns literal 1 with no mutation path (L243-245); included in state snapshot. Test asserts ===1 before/after register, after tick, and via state; artifact countdownDemo shows physicsTimeScale:1 across all 5 frames. Freeze is render-only (freezesMeshes flag), never a sim time-scale => fixed-timestep determinism/replay untouched.
6) Tunability — CONFIRMED. DEFAULT_CONFIG (heavyThreshold/band1/band2/maxFrames/eliminationFrames/eliminationMinIntensity) + constructor overrides + setConfig() runtime setter. Tests cover both (constructor heavyThreshold:0.5 gates 0.4 to none; setConfig band1:0.9 remaps 0.7=>1 frame).
7) No per-tick logging — CONFIRMED. tick() contains no console call (L204-212); lastImpact set only in registerImpact (L163), never in tick (test asserts lastImpact identity unchanged across tick). Spy test: 0 console.log/warn/error across register + 200 ticks.

ARTIFACT — hit-stop-decision-matrix.json: config block, 8-row input->decision matrix (0.10/focused=>none; 0.25=>freeze/1; 0.60=>freeze/2; 0.90/focused=>freeze/3; 0.90/shared-race=>camera-punch/3; 0.05/shared-race+elim=>camera-punch/3 intensity 0.8; default-context 0.90=>camera-punch/3) and a 3-frame countdown demo (3->2->1->0, physicsTimeScale 1 throughout). It is generated BY the unit test (writeFileSync), so my rerun regenerated it fresh and consistent.

TEST QUALITY: the 26 tests are genuine behavioral assertions (threshold gating, band mapping incl. boundaries, countdown exactness, cap enforcement under adversarial config, refresh-not-stack, context/elimination policy, physicsTimeScale invariance, tunability, determinism, console-spy). Not vacuous.

RESIDUAL NOTES (non-blocking):
- The evidence-artifact test performs filesystem writes (writeFileSync) inside a unit test — unusual but intentional and harmless for tuning evidence; deterministic output.
- Phase 2 (RenderSystem camera-punch + per-vehicle mesh-hold hooks, GameHost registration, HitStopSystem wiring, e2e/visual proof) remains correctly HELD pending render-overlap release; NOT in scope for this validation.

CONCLUSION: Phase 1 core is deterministic, frame-capped 1-3, refresh-not-stack, context-correct (no global slow-mo), physics-safe (timeScale fixed at 1), tunable, and free of per-tick logging; scope stayed strictly within the allowed new files with zero touches to guarded files. Fresh vitest 26/26 pass. PHASE 1 VALIDATION PASS. Not closing the bead; Phase 2 remains held.

Implementation plan if coordinator accepts these fallback reservations:
1. Build SmashCalloutOverlay as an injected, DOM-only host overlay with sanitized textContent, capped queue/cooldown, no innerHTML for player text, no per-frame logging, no timers except a bounded hide timeout/transition timer.
2. Subscribe only to damage:destroyed; ignore light damage. Expose diagnostics: lastEvent, visible, queue length, displayed text, source event count.
3. Style in the module-owned style tag to avoid host.css conflicts: hard ink border, matte sticker surface, loud player/danger color, no blur/glass, pointer-events none, aria-live polite.
4. Bootstrap from host entry after GameHost init; do not instantiate on /player, do not add phone/controller renderer or controls.
5. Unit tests: text formatting/sanitization, victim fallback naming, optional attacker/victim formatting when payload includes both, queue cap/no unbounded stacking, hide lifecycle with fake timers, no forbidden source patterns.
6. E2E/browser proof: host page initializes overlay; inject eventBus.emit('damage:destroyed', synthetic payload) or call test hook; assert visible text, screenshot artifact, diagnostics, no canvas/world changes beyond existing host, no overlap with lobby-as-world/leader-danger tests.

I can implement the overlay so it supports attackerName/attackerId when present and falls back to victim-only copy, but full attacker attribution likely requires touching DamageSystem/weapon source attribution or GameHost event enrichment, which is not in the non-overlapping safe path.

title: G2: Perf spike - calibrate adaptive-quality ladder for the grade
description: Ship ALL grade effects; do NOT cut by default. Spike measures per-effect GPU cost and per-tier fps on worst-case supported host hardware (old laptop / weak desktop driving the shared screen) and, separately, weak Remote viewer devices where Remote mode renders locally. Use the measurements to CALIBRATE the adaptive ladder (1.9): hardware-detection heuristic, dynamic resolution scaling (target native, drop internal res gracefully to hold fps), and the order in which heavier effects (vertex-snap/affine 1.8, then dither/grain) shed. Also locks the tone-mapping decision, including whether to skip ACES. Output = cost table + degrade-ladder thresholds + tone-map decision. Resolves docs/design/00 open Qs 4&5. Coordinate with `br-captain-call-architecture-hardening-woq.2` render backend.
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Benchmark artifact: checked-in table or report records per-effect GPU/frame cost for the committed grade stack on at least one worst-case supported host profile and one normal host profile; Remote weak-device measurements are separate and must not gate Local host play.
- Ladder decision: adaptive-quality thresholds specify hardware detection inputs, dynamic-resolution steps, effect-shedding order, minimum acceptable readability, and recovery/hysteresis behavior after pressure drops.
- Visual proof: screenshots or captured frames compare native/full-grade, degraded ladder tiers, and fallback path for lobby, race, derby, and high-player host scenes.
- Tone-map proof: decision log records ACES/skip-ACES outcome with visual comparison and why it matches the Skip Bin Arcade grade without crushing player readability.
- Test/profiling proof: automated smoke or documented manual harness records no blank canvas, no shader errors, and stable backend metadata while toggling grade tiers.
- Architecture guard: Local phones/controllers never become the host-renderer performance target; host rendering budgets apply to the shared-screen host, while Remote viewers degrade independently.
notes: Release manager blocked: dirty tree with 49 changed files prevents safe scoping of this bead's validated changes. Awaiting coordinator guidance on file ownership across beads or alternative release strategy.
close_reason: Completed and independently validated: fresh validator Russell reran npm run build and the grade Playwright evidence command, which passed 12/12; verified RenderSystem grade APIs, NoToneMapping/skip-ACES diagnostics, repaired waitForFunction timeout forms, representative timing-band doc framing, 11 nonblank 1280x720 screenshots, 3 diagnostics JSON files with real WebGL/tier/timing/tracked-vehicle values, and Local-vs-Remote render-target guard.
