comments.author: NavyDune
comments.text:
TO: Coordinator / Fresh Validator
FROM: NavyDune (Worker)
BEAD: br-skip-bin-arcade-design-language-5k3.3
THREAD: P1.1 Low-res render target + nearest-neighbor upscale
DATE: 2026-06-30
STATUS: 🟢 READY FOR FRESH VALIDATION (do not close; awaiting validator PASS)

---

SCOPE CHANGED
=============
Delivered the actual low-res internal render target + nearest-neighbor CRISP
upscale. G2 (5k3.2, closed) calibrated the grade ladder and added
resolutionScale->setPixelRatio plumbing, but never shipped the crisp upscale,
and on high-DPR panels its dpr*scale math kept "low" tiers above CSS-native so
they never actually looked low-res.

- static/css/host.css
  #game-container canvas now image-rendering: pixelated (+ crisp-edges,
  -moz-crisp-edges, -webkit-optimize-contrast, optimizeSpeed fallbacks).
  Nearest-neighbor upscale instead of the browser's default bilinear blur.

- static/js/systems/RenderSystem.js
  * _resolveEffectivePixelRatio(): a sub-native resolutionScale is now a
    fraction of CSS (logical) pixels, NOT device pixels. So 0.85/0.7/0.55 render
    genuinely below CSS-native on EVERY devicePixelRatio (dpr=1 TV and dpr=2
    laptop alike). The native tier (scale>=1) still renders up-to-native, capped
    at maxPixelRatio=2 -- "the low-res look is the floor, not a fixed value"
    (docs/design/02-design-language.md).
  * _applyResolutionScale(): pushes the new pixel ratio to the EffectComposer,
    which otherwise caches its construction-time ratio and would ignore tier
    resolution changes on the post-processed path.
  * _applyPixelatedUpscale(): also sets the canvas image-rendering inline, so the
    crisp grade holds even when the stylesheet is absent (test harnesses).
  * _getRenderTargetMetrics() + a renderTarget block in getGradeDiagnostics():
    exposes internalWidth/Height, displayWidth/Height, upscaleFactor, isUpscaled,
    imageRendering, crispUpscale, devicePixelRatio -- inspectable proof.

NOT changed: grade-tier resolutionScale config values (G2-calibrated), tone-map
decision, or any post-pass effect (those are P1.2/P1.3/P1.5).

EVIDENCE
========
Command: npm run build            -> OK (built in ~4.5s).
Command: npx playwright test tests/e2e/pixelation-upscale.spec.ts -> 5/5 pass.
Command: npx playwright test tests/e2e/grade-ladder.spec.ts       -> 3/3 pass.
Command: npx playwright test visual-effects + console-errors      -> 9/9 pass.
  (console-errors green => no per-frame logging introduced.)

Render-scale diagnostics (.ntm/evidence/5k3.3/dpr1-render-target.json):
  host-native   internal 1280x720  upscale 1.00  isUpscaled false  (1:1 native)
  host-balanced internal 1088x612  upscale 1.18  isUpscaled true
  host-degraded internal  896x503  upscale 1.43  isUpscaled true
  host-fallback internal  704x396  upscale 1.82  isUpscaled true

High-DPR edge case (dpr2-render-target.json, deviceScaleFactor=2):
  Sub-native tiers STILL 1088/896/704 px < 1280 display (genuinely low-res).
  Old dpr*scale code would have produced 2176/1792/1408 px (>= native) -- the
  exact regression this bead fixes. Native supersamples to 2560 (up-to-native).

Resize edge case (resize-render-target.json):
  host-degraded 1280->900 viewport: internal 896->630 (= round(900*0.7)),
  still isUpscaled true. Internal target recomputes proportionally.

FPS / frame-timing (frame-timing.json), sustained ~1.2s burst per tier:
  internalPixels 921600 -> 665856 -> 450688 -> 278784 (monotonic shed).
  avg render ms 0.32 / 0.32 / 0.23 / 0.15  -- all << 16.6ms (60fps budget).
  frameCount ~145/tier (loop genuinely advancing, not a frozen frame).

Non-blank visual proof:
  fallback-variance.json: luminance variance 321 (>5 threshold; not blank).
  Screenshots dpr1-{native,balanced,degraded,fallback}.png: world track lines
  visibly chunkier as the internal target drops, while the HTML HUD/QR overlay
  stays crisp (canvas-only grade, matching the readability-override intent).

Evidence dir: .ntm/evidence/5k3.3/  (4 PNG + 5 JSON)

EDGE CASES COVERED
==================
- devicePixelRatio = 1 (real TV/monitor host) and = 2 (retina dev laptop).
- Window resize recomputes the internal buffer.
- Post-processed (composer on) and direct render paths both apply the target.

NOT TESTED
==========

  Chromium; avg ms is a lower bound). The adaptive controller that drives tier
  selection under live pressure is a separate bead (5k3.39); this bead only
  delivers the target + crisp upscale mechanism it will steer.

PRODUCT INVARIANTS
==================
Pure host-render change; does not touch joins/results/maps/race/derby flow or
room lifecycle. Architecture guard intact: the shared-screen host is the render
target; Local controllers stay HUD-only (unmodeled here).

comments.author: NavyDune
comments.text:
TO: Coordinator / Fresh Validator
FROM: NavyDune (Worker)
BEAD: br-skip-bin-arcade-design-language-5k3.3
THREAD: P1.1 Low-res render target + nearest-neighbor upscale
DATE: 2026-06-30
STATUS: 🟢 READY FOR FRESH VALIDATION (do not close; awaiting validator PASS)

---

SCOPE CHANGED (3 reserved files only)
=====================================
Delivered the low-res internal render target + nearest-neighbor CRISP upscale.
G2 (5k3.2, closed) calibrated the grade ladder and added resolutionScale->
setPixelRatio plumbing, but never shipped the crisp upscale, and its dpr*scale
math kept "low" tiers above CSS-native on high-DPR panels so they never looked
low-res there.

- static/css/host.css
  #game-container canvas: image-rendering: pixelated (+ crisp-edges,
  -moz-crisp-edges, -webkit-optimize-contrast, optimizeSpeed fallbacks).
  Nearest-neighbor upscale instead of the browser's default bilinear blur.

- static/js/systems/RenderSystem.js
  * _resolveEffectivePixelRatio(): a sub-native resolutionScale is now a
    fraction of CSS (logical) pixels, NOT device pixels. 0.85/0.7/0.55 render
    genuinely below CSS-native on EVERY devicePixelRatio (dpr=1 TV and dpr=2
    laptop). Native (scale>=1) renders up-to-native, capped at maxPixelRatio=2
    -- "the low-res look is the floor, not a fixed value" (design 02).
  * _applyResolutionScale(): pushes the new pixel ratio to the EffectComposer
    via setPixelRatio() so the post-processed render target dimensions follow
    the selected internal scale (the composer otherwise caches its
    construction-time ratio and ignores tier res changes).
  * _applyPixelatedUpscale(): also sets canvas image-rendering inline, so the
    crisp grade holds even when the stylesheet is absent (test harnesses).
  * _getRenderTargetMetrics() + a self-contained renderTarget block in
    getGradeDiagnostics(): displayWidth/Height, internalWidth/Height,
    resolutionScale, effectivePixelRatio, devicePixelRatio, upscaleFactor,
    isUpscaled, imageRendering, crispUpscale.

- tests/e2e/pixelation-upscale.spec.ts (NEW): 5 deterministic tests. The
  non-blank check retries variance over a few frames to remove a
  transient-blank-frame screenshot race (capture artefact, not a blank world).

NOT changed: grade-tier resolutionScale config values (G2-calibrated), tone-map
decision, or any post-pass effect (P1.2/P1.3/P1.5).

COMMANDS + OUTPUTS
==================
$ npm run build
  -> "built in ~3s", exit 0.

$ npx playwright test tests/e2e/pixelation-upscale.spec.ts --workers=1 --reporter=line
  -> 5 passed (~16s). Re-ran twice consecutively: 5/5 both times (no flake).

$ npx playwright test tests/e2e/grade-ladder.spec.ts --workers=1 --reporter=line
  -> 3 passed (regression: getGradeDiagnostics change is backward compatible).

(Earlier broader run: visual-effects + console-errors 9/9; console-errors green
 => no per-frame logging introduced.)

DIAGNOSTIC SAMPLE (internal < CSS display on DPR 1 AND DPR 2)
============================================================
.ntm/evidence/5k3.3/dpr1-render-target.json (CSS display 1280x720, dpr=1):
  host-native   internal 1280x720  effPR 1.00  upscale 1.00  isUpscaled false
  host-balanced internal 1088x612  effPR 0.85  upscale 1.18  isUpscaled true
  host-degraded internal  896x503  effPR 0.70  upscale 1.43  isUpscaled true
  host-fallback internal  704x396  effPR 0.55  upscale 1.82  isUpscaled true
  all: imageRendering "pixelated", crispUpscale true.

.ntm/evidence/5k3.3/dpr2-render-target.json (deviceScaleFactor=2):
  host-native   internal 2560  (supersamples up-to-native, isUpscaled false)
  host-balanced internal 1088  < 1280 display  isUpscaled true
  host-degraded internal  896  < 1280 display  isUpscaled true
  host-fallback internal  704  < 1280 display  isUpscaled true
  Old dpr*scale would have produced 2176/1792/1408 (>= native) -- the exact
  regression this bead fixes.

Resize (.ntm/evidence/5k3.3/resize-render-target.json):
  host-degraded, 1280->900 viewport: internal 896->630 (= round(900*0.7)),
  still isUpscaled true. Internal target recomputes proportionally.

FPS / frame-timing (.ntm/evidence/5k3.3/frame-timing.json), ~1.2s burst/tier:
  internalPixels 921600 -> 665856 -> 450688 -> 278784 (monotonic shed).
  avg render ms ~0.27 / 0.27 / 0.18 / 0.13, all << 16.6ms (60fps budget).
  frameCount ~145/tier (loop genuinely advancing).

NON-BLANK + CRISP PROOF
=======================
fallback-variance.json: luminance variance 321 (>5 threshold; not blank).
computed CSS image-rendering asserted == "pixelated" per tier in the spec.
Screenshots dpr1-{native,balanced,degraded,fallback}.png: world track lines
visibly chunkier as the internal target drops; HTML HUD/QR overlay stays crisp
(canvas-only grade, matching the readability-override intent).

Evidence dir: .ntm/evidence/5k3.3/  (4 PNG + 5 JSON)

EDGE CASES COVERED
==================
- devicePixelRatio = 1 (real TV/monitor host) and = 2 (retina dev laptop).
- Window resize recomputes the internal buffer.
- Post-processed (composer on) and direct render paths both apply the target.
- MIN_RENDER_SCALE floor respected (lowest tier 0.55 > 0.5 floor).
- Transient-blank-frame screenshot race hardened in the non-blank test.

HONEST GAPS / NOT TESTED
========================
- Real worst-case old-laptop GPU fps: the runner is GPU-accelerated headless
  Chromium, so avg ms is a lower bound, not a worst-case host measurement. The
  adaptive controller that selects tiers under live pressure is a separate bead
  (5k3.39); this bead delivers only the target + crisp upscale it will steer.
- Firefox/Safari image-rendering fallbacks are shipped but only Chromium is
  exercised in CI; the computed value asserted in-test is "pixelated".

PRODUCT INVARIANTS
==================
Pure host-render change; does not touch joins/results/maps/race/derby flow or
room lifecycle. Architecture guard intact: the shared-screen host is the render
target; Local controllers stay HUD-only (unmodeled here).

- static/js/systems/RenderSystem.js

title: P7.0: Catch-up policy DECIDED - implicit proximity, no explicit rubber-banding
description: DECISION 2026-06-29: NO explicit rubber-banding system (no speed manipulation, no Blue-Shell-style position-targeting catch-up). A LITTLE catch-up to keep it interesting is fine, but achieve "keep them together" through SOFT/IMPLICIT mechanics: (1) arena/track design that funnels cars back together (chokepoints, loops, re-merging shortcuts, short tracks); (2) the shrinking derby arena (already exists) - forces proximity without touching speed; (3) make the LEADER a target (weapons/attention converge) so being ahead carries risk and the pack naturally compresses; (4) short rounds so gaps never grow large. At most a subtle position-weighted item luck - keep it barely noticeable. Implementation lives in 7.1 + level-design (coord ...3xv.13 curated maps, ...3xv.9 comeback tuning, map-authoring epic).
close_reason: Decided: no explicit rubber-banding; keep-them-together via level design + shrinking arena + leader-as-target + short rounds

title: P7.1: Keep losers engaged - soft proximity mechanics + fast re-entry
description: Per the P7.0 decision (no explicit rubber-banding): keep eliminated/last players in via fast derby re-entry / spectator-with-agency AND soft proximity (shrinking arena, leader-as-target, short rounds, funneling layouts) rather than speed catch-up. Eliminated players are not just waiting. Coord w/ ...3xv.8 derby late-joins, ...3xv.9 comeback tuning.
close_reason: Implemented loser engagement soft arena pressure and host banner; BlueLake supplied unit/build/E2E/artifact evidence and StormyMill posted PASS-FULL fresh validation (Agent Mail msg 2703).

Manual settings win over the auto heuristic. Extends existing visual toggles.

title: P7.3: Lo-fi audio pass (crunchy engines, smash crunch, tape hiss)
description:
Audio matches the camcorder world; smash audio lands the hit.

Scope:
- Use the sound-design pipeline from `br-sound-design-pipeline-64l5`, especially the new-sound gate `br-sound-design-pipeline-64l5.5`, rather than adding ad hoc files or oscillator snippets.
- Bring engines, smash/collision crunch, tape hiss/ambience, boost/wheelie accents, weapon/pickup cues, UI earcons, and result/lobby stingers into the lo-fi Skip Bin Arcade grade.
- Keep readability in a noisy room: important state-change sounds must cut through without turning into constant harsh noise or masking player conversation.
- Respect audio voice budgets for high-player Local mode and Remote viewer degradation.
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Pipeline gate: changed sounds pass `br-sound-design-pipeline-64l5.5` with manifest entries, lab evidence bundles, objective metrics, and human/manual listening notes where taste matters.
- Style proof: A/B evidence shows the new audio matches the lo-fi/camcorder world without making key gameplay state harder to hear.
- Gameplay proof: collision/smash, boost/wheelie, weapon/pickup, UI/result/lobby sounds are heard in representative game or lab scenarios and mapped to real events, not placeholders.
- Budget proof: high-player audio voice counts/cooldowns/priority stealing are documented and do not create runaway overlapping sounds.
- Regression proof: existing music unlock, volume sliders, engine sound, and ducking still work.

title: P1.9: Adaptive quality controller (HW detect + dynamic res scaling + effect tiering)
description: Ship the full grade, auto-degrade by hardware. (a) Hardware/runtime-fps detection heuristic; (b) dynamic resolution scaling - render up to NATIVE res when the host can, drop internal res gracefully to hold target fps; (c) effect tiering - shed heaviest effects first (vertex-snap/affine, then grain/dither cost) per the G2 ladder. Requires every grade effect be runtime-toggleable with an intensity param (architectural constraint on P1.1-1.6,1.8). Manual override layer = 7.2 sits on top. Can be done whenever - NOT a blocker for shipping the grade at a sensible default. [calibrated by G2]

comments.author: cdilga
comments.text:
VALIDATION PASS — br-skip-bin-arcade-design-language-5k3.4 (ClaudeDitherValidator, pane 0.1, no-edit audit)

P1.2 posterize + ordered (Bayer) dither post pass is implemented, tunable, and composes correctly with the P1.1 low-res upscale (5k3.3). No files edited, bead not closed, unrelated dirty work preserved. Note: work is present as working-tree changes (uncommitted), not yet committed.

IMPLEMENTATION (inspected):
- static/js/shaders/ColorGradingShader.js: real posterize (uniform posterizeBandCount, levels=max(2,bands)) + ordered 4x4 Bayer matrix bayer4x4() with the correct threshold set (0,8,2,10 / 12,4,14,6 / 3,11,1,9 / 15,7,13,5 over 16), applied in posterizeWithDither() using gl_FragCoord.xy and a tunable ditherStrength; plus skip-bin palette grade + vignette. Defaults bands=7.0, dither=0.55.
- static/js/systems/RenderSystem.js: pass wired into EffectComposer after bloom (addPass colorGradingPass, lines ~699-707). Runtime-tunable per adaptive tier: native/balanced/degraded/fallback = bands 7/6/5/0, dither 0.55/0.5/0.42/0 (uniforms updated at lines ~1680-1681). Live diagnostics via getGradeDiagnostics()/listGradeTiers() expose colorGradingStyle, posterizeBandCount, ditherStrength, ditherPattern='bayer-4x4'.
- Low-res composition: composer renders at reduced effectivePixelRatio (setPixelRatio + setSize(...,false)) and the canvas is nearest-neighbor upscaled (_markCanvasForCrispUpscale, imageRendering:pixelated). So posterize+dither run at the low-res target and upscale chunky — they compose with, not break, 5k3.3.

COMMANDS + RESULTS:
- npm run build -> ✓ built in ~3s (dirty shader+RenderSystem bundle cleanly; only pre-existing chunk-size/CJS warnings).
- npx playwright test tests/e2e/visual-effects.spec.ts --grep "host-grade ladder diagnostics" -> 1 passed (3.6s), live against current code. Asserts (from live render.getGradeDiagnostics()): tiers==[host-native,host-balanced,host-degraded,host-fallback], toneMapping skip-aces/NoToneMapping, colorGradingStyle=='skip-bin-arcade-posterize-dither', ditherPattern=='bayer-4x4', posterizeBandCount>=5, ditherStrength>0, screenshotLength>10000. These are wired to live uniforms, not hardcoded.

INSPECTABLE ARTIFACT PROOF (artifacts/br-skip-bin-arcade-design-language-5k3.4/):
- race-grade-diagnostics.json / lobby-grade-diagnostics.json / derby-high-player-diagnostics.json: per-tier bands/dither with bayer-4x4, and renderTarget metrics proving coexistence with upscale:
  host-native  bands=7 dither=0.55 upscaled=false internal=1280x720 crisp=true pixelated
  host-balanced bands=6 dither=0.5  upscaled=true(1.18x) internal=1088x612 crisp=true pixelated
  host-degraded bands=5 dither=0.42 upscaled=true(1.43x) internal=896x503  crisp=true pixelated
  host-fallback bands=0 dither=0    pattern=null (grade fully disabled at fallback) upscaled=true(1.82x) crisp=true
  -> tunable band count confirmed (7/6/5/0); ordered dither + low-res upscale coexist; crispUpscale stays true.

VISUAL PIXEL PROOF (read the PNGs):
- race-host-native.png: muted skip-bin green world with pronounced ordered (Bayer) stipple breaking up posterized color bands; banded warm glow around the car.
- race-host-degraded.png: same ordered dither but COARSER/chunkier cells and a pixelated car from nearest-neighbor upscale of the 896x503 low-res buffer -> dither survives the low-res upscale as chunky pixels (signature lo-fi look), 5k3.3 intact.

SCOPE/HYGIENE: no files edited by me. Started the Flask server only to run the focused E2E, then stopped it (restored to down). dist/ regenerated by build (expected). Unrelated dirty files (host/render/UI/design WIP) left untouched.

RESIDUAL NOTES (non-blocking):
- Work is uncommitted working-tree; coordinator should ensure it lands. Diagnostics PNGs/JSON are dated 2026-06-30 but the live test re-confirms current code matches them.
- vignette lives in this shader (arguably 5k3.8 P1.6 scope) but does not affect P1.2 acceptance.
- Full 4-player E2E suite not run (only the focused grade test); acceptance for this bead is fully covered by that test + diagnostics + visual proof.

VERDICT: VALIDATION PASS. World color banding + ordered Bayer dither exist, are tunable (per-tier band count/strength, fallback disables), and do not break the prior low-res nearest-neighbor upscale — proven by live test, inspectable per-tier diagnostics, and native-vs-upscaled pixel screenshots. Deferring close to the coordinator.

comments.author: ClaudeMaterialValidator
comments.text:
VALIDATION BLOCKED — ClaudeMaterialValidator (no-edit/no-spawn audit; Agent Mail down, coordinating via br).

Acceptance under test: "Cars/track/props no longer MeshStandardMaterial; toon ramp or flat; no plastic spec."

WHAT PASSES (evidence):
- Cars fully converted. static/js/resources/VehicleFactory.js creates ALL meshes via this.materialFactory.createMaterial(): body/roof/wheels type:'toon' (L133/154/172), headlights type:'flat' (L262). Zero MeshStandardMaterial.
- Track + track-props fully converted. static/js/resources/TrackFactory.js routes every material through materialFactory.createMaterial() (17 call sites, all type:'toon' — L112/176/226/251/287/315/344/412/459/490/611/619/667/700/723/751): ground, walls, start line, ramps/rims/rings. Zero MeshStandardMaterial.
- MaterialFactory (static/js/resources/MaterialFactory.js) emits MeshBasicMaterial (flat) / MeshToonMaterial (toon) only — no metalness/roughness, matte. replacePBRMaterials() correctly targets isMeshStandardMaterial.
- DamageSystem.js and RenderSystem.js create no MeshStandardMaterial. npm run build succeeds (vite ✓ built in 5.21s).

BLOCKERS (exact files/lines needing repair):
1. Weapon props still PBR MeshStandardMaterial — static/js/systems/WeaponSystem.js:
   - L672 _createPickupMesh(): new THREE.MeshStandardMaterial({ ..., metalness: 0.5, roughness: 0.3 }) — this is a literal plastic-spec PBR material on an in-world collectible box. Directly violates both 'props no longer MeshStandardMaterial' AND 'no plastic spec' (design 02 cohesion checklist #2).
   - L1558 _createProjectileMesh(): MeshStandardMaterial.
   - L1577 _createMineMesh(): MeshStandardMaterial.
   Pickups/mines are world props; per design-02 they are 'loud/danger' diegetic emissive, but must be matte emissive (MeshBasic/MeshToon + emissive), NOT PBR with metalness/roughness. Fix: route through MaterialFactory (flat + emissive).

2. No automated test asserts the material conversion. Coordinator required 'convincing tests plus visual/material evidence.' The two NEW unit tests on this branch are unrelated to materials: tests/unit/safe-rendering.test.js (SafeTextRenderer/XSS) and tests/unit/renderer-backend.test.js (WebGPU/WebGL backend). grep of tests/ finds no MaterialFactory / MeshToon / 'no MeshStandardMaterial in scene' assertion. Add a unit/E2E guard that (a) MaterialFactory returns MeshToon/MeshBasic and (b) no scene mesh is isMeshStandardMaterial after track+vehicles+pickups build.

3. Toon-ramp assignment bug — static/js/resources/MaterialFactory.js:56-59. The guard conflates 'create ramp once' with 'assign ramp':
     if (typeof document !== 'undefined' && !MaterialFactory.toonRamp) {
         MaterialFactory.toonRamp = this.createToonRamp();
         toonMaterial.gradientMap = MaterialFactory.toonRamp;   // only reached on FIRST toon material
     }
   After the first toon material sets the static ramp, !MaterialFactory.toonRamp is false, so every subsequent MeshToonMaterial (nearly all cars/track meshes) is returned with gradientMap=null and never gets the crisp 2-band '#444/#ccc' ramp. Result: only 1 mesh in the whole scene uses the authored toon ramp. Fix: assign gradientMap on every toon material; only the createToonRamp() call should be gated by !toonRamp.

Note on 'props' scope: cars/track pass cleanly; the sole PBR remnants are the weapon pickup/projectile/mine meshes. If coordinator rules weapon meshes out of scope for THIS bead (separate weapon-VFX bead), items 2+3 still block a PASS. I did not capture live browser/screenshot evidence (no-spawn + heavy server bring-up); code + build evidence stands on its own for the blockers above. Not closing — leaving IN_PROGRESS for VioletShift.

No Phong/Lambert/Physical anywhere. No live specular.
- npm run build => vite ✓ built in 2.58s (chunk-size warning only).

BLOCKER-BY-BLOCKER VERIFICATION:
1) Weapon props PBR/plastic-spec removed — CONFIRMED. static/js/systems/WeaponSystem.js imports MaterialFactory (L20), instantiates this.materialFactory (L74). _createPickupMesh (L679) => createMaterial({type:'flat'}) (unlit MeshBasic); _createProjectileMesh (L1563) => createMaterial({type:'flat'}); _createMineMesh (L1582) => createMaterial({type:'toon', emissive:color, emissiveIntensity:0.5}) (dark matte toon + diegetic glow). No metalness/roughness on any weapon prop. Shield/zone meshes use plain MeshBasicMaterial (unlit/matte) — also non-PBR, acceptable.
2) Toon-ramp assignment fix — CONFIRMED. MaterialFactory.createToonMaterial (L48-69) now caches ramp creation behind `if (!MaterialFactory.toonRamp)` but assigns `toonMaterial.gradientMap` in a SEPARATE `if (MaterialFactory.toonRamp)` block, so EVERY toon material gets the shared ramp (pre-fix, 2nd+ materials got null). Unit test creates 3 toon materials, asserts all three receive gradientMap, all share ONE instance, and CanvasTexture is constructed exactly once (caching preserved).
3) Automated tests added and MEANINGFUL (not vacuous) — CONFIRMED. tests/unit/material-conversion.test.js:
   - Positive type assertions: flat=>isMeshBasicMaterial===true, toon=>isMeshToonMaterial===true (real constructor selection, not just falsy checks).
   - No metalness/roughness forwarded (FakeMaterial does Object.assign(this, params), so any leaked PBR param would surface).
   - Flat carries no emissive (guards the "don't forward emissive to unlit MeshBasic" behavior).
   - Toon-ramp regression: 3 materials all get shared gradientMap + single CanvasTexture — targets the exact filed bug.
   - Runtime harness EXECUTES real production code: WeaponSystem.prototype._createPickupMesh/_createProjectileMesh/_createMineMesh via .call({materialFactory}); asserts MeshBasic/MeshToon, not PBR, no metalness, and mine emissive === passed idle color '#FF0000'.
   - Source-scan guard over VehicleFactory/TrackFactory/WeaponSystem asserting /new THREE.MeshStandardMaterial/ has no match.

Cars/track (already clean per prior audit) re-confirmed: no PBR constructors remain anywhere in static/js.

RESIDUAL RISKS (all non-blocking):
- The isMeshStandardMaterial===falsy assertions are individually weak (the test's fake THREE defines no MeshStandardMaterial class), but each is paired with a strong positive type assertion and backed by the source-scan guard + my independent repo-wide grep, so the suite as a whole is not vacuous.

Cosmetic cleanup opportunity only.

- No live in-browser screenshot of the toon ramp rendering was captured (no-spawn constraint, consistent with prior audit). The unit test proves the gradientMap-assignment mechanism that the ramp bug broke; visual confirmation of the 2-band ramp on-screen is deferred. vehicle-model-validation skill was considered and does NOT apply here: this is a material-shading language change on existing meshes, not a GLB model import/replacement/normalization/catalog/rigging/collider task.

Conclusion: all three blockers are genuinely repaired, tests are meaningful and pass (12/12), build is clean, and no MeshStandardMaterial / PBR plastic-spec remains on cars, track, or weapon props anywhere in static/js. VALIDATION PASS. Leaving the actual close to owner/coordinator per no-close constraint.

title: P1.4: Invert bloom -> emissive-only diegetic glow
description: Full-frame bloom gone; bloom gated to neon signage/headlights/pickups only.

comments.author: cdilga
comments.text: Coordinator planning claim: assigning existing Claude pane 0.1 as ClaudeBloomPlan for no-edit planning first. Swarm remains capped at five panes total including the user pane; no spawn. Agent Mail MCP is unavailable, so coordinate here with br comments. Read AGENTS.md, README.md, this bead, design-language docs, current dirty status/diffs, and material bead 5k3.5 close notes. Post a conflict-aware plan for emissive-only diegetic bloom: likely file scope, how to avoid full-frame bloom, how to keep neon signage/headlights/pickups bloom-eligible, tests/visual evidence, and exact files that must wait for active name-tag/analytics/render/UI work. Do not edit files until coordinator explicitly releases the overlap.

comments.author: ClaudeBloomPlan
comments.text:
IMPLEMENTATION PLAN (NO EDITS YET) — ClaudeBloomPlan for br-skip-bin-arcade-design-language-5k3.6 "Invert bloom -> emissive-only diegetic glow". Planning only; Agent Mail down, coordinating via br. Not editing/closing until coordinator releases the overlapping files.

ACCEPTANCE: "Full-frame bloom gone; bloom gated to neon signage/headlights/pickups only." Grounded in docs/design/02-design-language.md L44 (INVERT maximal UnrealBloom -> faint emissive-only bleed; bloom for neon *signage*, not the whole frame), L67-69 + L108-111 + L212-214 (the ONLY bloom-eligible things: headlights, neon signage/track edges, weapon pickups/glows, DANGER #FF2E2E strips, fire). Depends on 5k3.5 (DONE/validated): all world materials are now flat MeshBasic / toon MeshToon, so we finally have a clean emissive/loud-color signal to gate on.

=== CURRENT STATE (what makes it "full-frame") ===
static/js/systems/RenderSystem.js._initPostProcessing() (~L709-760) builds ONE EffectComposer:
  RenderPass -> UnrealBloomPass(strength 1.5, radius 0.4, threshold 0.85) -> ColorGradingShader(posterize+dither+vignette) -> RGBShiftShader.
The UnrealBloomPass runs on the whole framebuffer (luminance threshold), so with unlit full-bright MeshBasic surfaces + NoToneMapping (L602) large areas exceed threshold and bloom -> the "expensive modern glow" the design wants gone. Grade tiers (HOST_GRADE_TIER_DEFINITIONS L30-98) only scale bloomStrength/threshold per tier; they never restrict WHICH objects bloom. _renderScene() (~L1663) just calls composer.render(). getRenderDiagnostics() (~L496-510) exposes postProcessing.passes.bloom + colorGradingStyle.

=== APPROACH: selective (layer-gated) bloom, two-composer ===
Replace the single full-frame pass with the canonical three.js selective-bloom pipeline:
1. BLOOM layer convention: reserve a THREE layer (e.g. BLOOM_LAYER = 1). A mesh blooms IFF it is on that layer.
2. bloomComposer (renderToScreen=false): RenderPass -> UnrealBloomPass. Before its render, darkenNonBloomed(): traverse scene; for every mesh NOT on BLOOM_LAYER, stash its material in a Map and swap to a shared black MeshBasicMaterial; after render, restoreMaterials(). Net: only flagged emissive objects contribute to the bloom target. (Alternative = camera.layers mask + separate clear, but material-darken is the proven path and preserves depth/composition.)
3. finalComposer (renderToScreen=true): RenderPass(full scene, all layers) -> mixPass (additive ShaderPass: baseTexture + bloomTexture from bloomComposer.renderTarget2.texture) -> ColorGradingShader(posterize/dither/vignette) -> RGBShiftShader. Keeping the grade AFTER the bloom composite so the faint glow still gets posterized/dithered into the camcorder grade (design wants glow to read *through* the grade, not float above it).
4. _renderScene(): darkenNonBloomed() -> bloomComposer.render() -> restoreMaterials() -> finalComposer.render(). Keep the try/catch fallback to plain renderer.render() on error.
5. Tune UnrealBloom DOWN to a faint bleed (design L44): drop strength ~1.5 -> ~0.6-0.8, keep threshold low (~0) since only pre-filtered emissive objects reach the bloom target — threshold no longer needs to reject the world.

=== HOW OBJECTS BECOME BLOOM-ELIGIBLE (data-driven, minimal factory churn) ===
Centralize detection in RenderSystem.addMesh() (single choke point; WeaponSystem/VehicleFactory/TrackFactory all register through render.addMesh) rather than spraying layers.enable across factories. Rule for enabling BLOOM_LAYER on a mesh + descendants:
  - material.emissiveIntensity > 0 (covers toon signage/track-edges/danger strips/mine glow — TrackFactory L177..L751 emissive fields, VehicleFactory L263-264 headlight emissive), OR
  - mesh.userData.bloomEligible === true (explicit opt-in for unlit MeshBasic loud props that have NO emissive channel: headlight lenses + weapon pickups/projectiles, which 5k3.5 made flat full-bright).
So the only factory edits needed are a one-line `userData.bloomEligible = true` at: VehicleFactory headlight mesh (_addLights ~L262), WeaponSystem _createPickupMesh (~L679) and _createProjectileMesh (~L1563). Everything else is caught by the emissive>0 rule with zero factory edits. A new shared module static/js/resources/bloomLayer.js exports BLOOM_LAYER + isBloomEligible(mesh) + enableBloom(object) so factories and RenderSystem share one source of truth (new file = zero merge conflict).

=== FILE SCOPE ===
NEW (no conflict, can start immediately even before release):
  - static/js/resources/bloomLayer.js — BLOOM_LAYER const + isBloomEligible()/enableBloom() helpers.
  - tests/unit/bloom-selective.test.js — unit guard (see tests below).
MUST WAIT FOR COORDINATOR RELEASE (all currently dirty from active work — see conflict map):
  - static/js/systems/RenderSystem.js  (+156 dirty: 5k3.3/5k3.4 low-res+posterize+dither+grade-tier work sits in the exact _initPostProcessing / grade-tier / getRenderDiagnostics region I must refactor). PRIMARY conflict.
  - static/js/shaders/ColorGradingShader.js (+95 dirty: 5k3.4 posterize/dither). I likely only READ it, but the mixPass ordering interacts with it; do not edit concurrently.
  - static/js/resources/VehicleFactory.js (dirty M) — 1-line headlight flag.
  - static/js/systems/WeaponSystem.js (dirty M, just settled by 5k3.5) — 2-line pickup/projectile flags.
  - static/js/resources/TrackFactory.js (dirty M) — expected NO edit (emissive rule covers it) but it's in the blast radius; confirm after release.
  - static/js/GameHost.js (+188 dirty: name-tag/analytics/perf-sample work) — READ-ONLY for me; it consumes getRenderDiagnostics (L1407, L1666). I must keep the diagnostics keys it reads stable, but must NOT edit it.
  - tests/e2e/visual-effects.spec.ts (dirty M) — needs new assertions; coordinate so I don't clobber in-flight edits.

=== CONFLICT-AVOIDANCE (name-tag / analytics / render / UI work) ===
- RenderSystem.js is the hot file: active 5k3.3/5k3.4 grade work AND my bloom refactor both live in _initPostProcessing/getRenderDiagnostics/grade-tier defs. Do NOT touch until coordinator confirms 5k3.3/5k3.4 have landed/committed. Sequence bloom AFTER posterize/dither so the pass chain is stable when I splice in the two-composer + mixPass.
- BACKWARD-COMPAT contract I must preserve so I don't break concurrent consumers/tests: keep postProcessing.composer non-null; keep postProcessing.passes.bloom present (visual-effects.spec.ts L125 asserts hasBloom===true; UnrealBloomPass now lives on bloomComposer but expose it on passes.bloom); keep passes.colorGrading + colorGradingStyle 'skip-bin-arcade-posterize-dither' + posterizeBandCount (spec L162-164); keep _applyGradeTierSettings() able to enable/disable + scale bloom per tier (bloom OFF tiers must skip the whole bloomComposer for perf). Add-only new diagnostics keys: selectiveBloom:true, fullFrameBloom:false, bloomLayer, bloomEligibleCount.
- GameHost/NetworkSystem/LobbyUI/RoomCodeOverlayUI dirty work is name-tag/analytics/UI and does NOT intersect the render pipeline except via getRenderDiagnostics (read-only) and perf-sample drawCalls (5k3 telemetry) — no edit needed there; I stay out.
- Weapon pickups restyle (5k3.33) DEPENDS on this bead and expects pickups to be "the bloomed loud layer" — my userData.bloomEligible flag on pickups is the seam they build on; I'll document the convention so 5k3.33 reuses it.

=== TESTS / VISUAL EVIDENCE ===
Unit (tests/unit/bloom-selective.test.js, vitest node env, fake THREE like material-conversion.test.js):
  - isBloomEligible(): true when material.emissiveIntensity>0; true when userData.bloomEligible; false for a plain flat MeshBasic world mesh (grimy ground/wall) -> proves the environment is EXCLUDED (the core "no full-frame" guarantee).
  - darkenNonBloomed()/restoreMaterials(): non-eligible meshes are swapped to black during bloom pass and byte-for-byte restored after (Map round-trip); eligible meshes keep their material.
  - enableBloom(object) sets BLOOM_LAYER on the object + all descendants.
  - Source-scan/behavior guard: pipeline builds TWO composers and the final chain contains a mix/composite pass (no single full-frame UnrealBloom feeding the screen directly).
E2E (extend tests/e2e/visual-effects.spec.ts after release, or a new tests/e2e/selective-bloom.spec.ts to avoid clobbering the dirty spec):
  - diagnostics.postProcessing.selectiveBloom===true, fullFrameBloom===false, hasBloom stays true.
  - Screenshot-based proof of the gate: sample pixels over a known grimy track/ground region -> assert NOT bloomed (low luma / no halo bleed); sample over a headlight or pickup -> assert bright halo present. Save artifact test-results/visual/selective-bloom-*.png (host-rendered; validator-visible).
  - console-errors.spec.ts must stay green (composer swap must not throw / no per-frame logs).
Build: npm run build (Vite dynamic-imports the postprocessing modules; confirm chunking still builds).
Perf note: two composers + full-scene RenderPass twice ~doubles scene draw cost on the host; must respect the grade ladder — on tiers with bloomEnabled:false skip bloomComposer entirely (single-composer fast path), and coordinate with 5k3.2 (perf spike) / 5k3.39 (adaptive quality) so selective bloom is a tier-gated effect, not always-on.

=== SEQUENCING / ASKS FOR COORDINATOR ===
1. Release order: confirm 5k3.3 (low-res target) + 5k3.4 (posterize/dither) are committed/landed before I refactor _initPostProcessing, so I splice into a stable pass chain.
2. Explicit release of RenderSystem.js + ColorGradingShader.js (read) + VehicleFactory.js + WeaponSystem.js + tests/e2e/visual-effects.spec.ts to ClaudeBloom(impl) with the dirty hunks preserved (I'll rebase my hunks onto whatever landed, not revert).
3. Meanwhile I can create bloomLayer.js + bloom-selective.test.js (new files, no conflict) to de-risk the seam.
4. Confirm whether to extend the dirty visual-effects.spec.ts or add a separate selective-bloom.spec.ts (my recommendation: separate spec to avoid clobbering active edits).

RESIDUAL RISKS: (a) UnrealBloom threshold semantics change (now pre-filtered by layer, threshold near 0) — verify faint-bleed look with a screenshot, not just presence. (b) transparent/additive effects (shield/zone MeshBasic, EMP rings, trails) may need explicit include/exclude decisions — design says restyle to chunky dithered sprites (5k3.17) NOT soft glow, so default them OFF the bloom layer unless flagged. (c) double RenderPass perf on low tiers — mitigated by tier-gated fast path. (d) darken/restore must be exception-safe so a throw mid-pass never leaves the scene stuck in black materials (restore in finally).

Not editing or closing. Awaiting coordinator release of the overlapping files.

Implemented in RenderSystem.js: far-plane capped at 600 (>skydome radius 500, <legacy 1000), FogExp2 colour == skyDome horizon band (0x4a2510) for a seamless dissolve (tier re-creation preserves it), sky dome camera-locked each frame, deliberate NoToneMapping exposed via getDiagnostics(). Fresh re-run of tests/e2e/fog-horizon.spec.ts -> 5/5 passing (fog==horizon, far<=600>domeRadius, dome-locked<1.0, toneMapping decision=skip-aces mode=NoToneMapping, fog.enabled, non-blank canvas). comments.author: OpusBackfill
comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED — automated E2E acceptance GREEN (tests/e2e/fog-horizon.spec.ts 5/5). Not self-closing; requesting fresh validation + visual sign-off.

Changes to static/js/systems/RenderSystem.js:
- Short draw distance: camera far-plane capped at SHORT_DRAW_DISTANCE=600 (was legacy 1000), still > sky-dome radius 500 so the dome never clips.
- Fog = sky-dome horizon band: scene.fog (FogExp2) colour now equals skyDome.material.uniforms.horizonColor (0x4a2510) so the world dissolves into the sky with no hard seam. Tier-gated fog re-creation also uses the horizon colour so a tier toggle never reintroduces a seam.
- Sky dome camera-locked each frame (render(): skyDome.position.copy(camera.position)) so the short far-plane is safe on large procedural circuits where the camera roams far from origin.
- Tone mapping stays deliberate NoToneMapping (per G2); exposed via new getDiagnostics() (alias of getGradeDiagnostics, now also reports fog.color) so the decision is inspectable.

Also fixed the spec's fog-type probe to use THREE's minification-safe .isFogExp2 flag (constructor.name is mangled by the production/dist minifier the E2E server serves).

Evidence: npx playwright test tests/e2e/fog-horizon.spec.ts -> 5 passed (fog==horizon, far<=600>domeRadius, dome camera-locked <1.0, toneMapping decision=skip-aces mode=NoToneMapping, fog.enabled, non-blank canvas). Full unit suite 1038 green; npm run build green; visual-effects.spec.ts still green.

Visual/aesthetic sign-off (does the dissolve read right per design G2) remains a human gate.

title: Sound design engineering pipeline and validation
description:
Create a real sound-engineering pipeline for Joystick Jammers instead of adding ad hoc samples or one-off oscillator snippets.

Problem this closes:
The repo already has audio infrastructure and useful experiments: `AudioSystem.js`, `audioManager.js`, procedural `EngineSynth`, music/SFX volume controls, hardcoded bundled samples, synthesized missile/explosion helpers, and design notes around jsfxr/sfxr, Web Audio, lo-fi grade, weapon sound problems, boost feel, and audio budgets. What is missing is a durable pipeline that tells agents how to research, author, audition, validate, bundle, route, and regression-test new sounds.

Owner clarification captured 2026-06-29:
- Add a deep research task on the pipeline of sound design, including libraries and methods for generating sounds.
- Create a reusable skill for Claude and Codex that encodes real sound design principles and gives the LLM qualitative feedback loops so it can reason about sounds it generated or modified.
- Ensure the project audio infrastructure can play whichever source strategy wins: bundled samples, procedural synth, MIDI-generated/rendered sounds, or a hybrid.
- New sounds need an audio generation, wiring, and validation pipeline, not taste-only commits.

Design intent:
Sound is a first-class feel channel for around-the-couch fun: speed, boost, wheelie, collision, weapon, pickup, result, lobby energy, and lo-fi world texture should all communicate state without muddying the room. The pipeline must support human taste/playtest gates while still giving agents objective artifacts they can inspect: waveforms, spectrograms, loudness/peak/crest factor, spectral centroid, transient envelope, duration/silence/clipping checks, event timing, voice counts, and A/B notes.
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Child coverage: research, runtime infrastructure, local sound lab, reusable Claude/Codex skill, and new-sound acceptance gate each have their own complete Beads and acceptance criteria.
- Existing audio work is wired: lo-fi audio pass, boost/wheelie audio cues, weapon/pickup audio, and around-couch audio budgets cannot close with ad hoc sound changes that bypass the pipeline.
- Validation model: the pipeline records both objective audio checks and qualitative/human playtest gates; it never pretends metrics alone prove taste.
- Repo fit: the approach respects no-CDN runtime rules, browser audio unlock policy, Local host-vs-controller roles, Remote viewer graceful degradation, and high-player voice-budget constraints.
- Graph hygiene: `br dep cycles --blocking-only --json` returns zero active blocking cycles after dependency edits.

title: SND research game-audio generation methods and validation pipeline
description:
Run a deep current-state research pass on practical game sound-design pipelines for this project.

Scope:
- Research real game-audio design principles relevant to arcade racers and party games: transient/body/tail layering, impact sweetening, engine loops/synthesis, UI earcons, boost ramps, weapon identity, pickup readability, loudness hierarchy, masking, fatigue, repetition variation, ducking, voice limiting, and room-social readability.
- Compare implementation methods: bundled authored samples, Web Audio procedural synthesis, jsfxr/sfxr-style parameter synthesis, Tone.js-style musical/sequencer tooling, MIDI composition/rendering, generated audio from external tools/skills, and hybrid pipelines.
- Evaluate current browser/runtime constraints: Web Audio unlock, `OfflineAudioContext` testability, decoding/bundling formats, mobile/browser autoplay policy, latency, memory, voice count, and no-CDN/runtime dependency rules.
- Produce a decision matrix for which sound families should use sample, synth, MIDI/rendered, or hybrid sources: engines, boost/wheelie, collisions, weapons, pickups, UI, music/stingers, ambience/tape hiss, crowd/result moments.
- Identify candidate libraries/tools and their licensing/maintenance/runtime cost. Do not add dependencies during research; recommend only after checking current docs and repo fit.
- Decide what belongs in normal CI, opt-in audio QA, manual playtest, and human taste gates.
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Research artifact: checked-in document summarizes current sources, links, candidate libraries/tools, licensing/dependency implications, and why each is or is not suitable for this repo.
- Decision matrix: every major sound family has a recommended source strategy: bundled sample, procedural synth, jsfxr/sfxr-style params, MIDI/rendered asset, hybrid, or defer.
- Validation plan: artifact defines objective metrics and qualitative review for each sound family, including what can be automated and what needs human ears/playtest.
- Runtime-fit proof: recommendations explicitly account for Web Audio unlock, `OfflineAudioContext`, no-CDN runtime, asset bundling, high-player voice budgets, Local host/controller roles, and Remote viewer degradation.
- Follow-up wiring: if a chosen library/dependency is recommended, create or update a specific implementation bead with tests and bundle-size/runtime-risk notes rather than silently adding it here.

title: SND audio runtime manifest and sample-synth-midi infrastructure
description:
Audit and harden the project runtime audio infrastructure so the final pipeline can support bundled samples, procedural synthesis, MIDI/rendered assets, or a hybrid.

Current starting point:
- `static/js/audioManager.js` hardcodes music and SFX asset paths, owns `AudioContext`, volume, ducking, sample decoding, synthesized missile/explosion helpers, and cached noise via `Math.random`.
- `static/js/systems/AudioSystem.js` maps game events to audio, but many weapon/pickup/checkpoint events reuse placeholders.
- `static/js/audio/EngineSynth.js` provides a procedural engine synth.
- `static/audio/music/**` and `static/audio/sfx/**` contain bundled mp3 assets.

Scope:
- Introduce a sound manifest/spec layer for every playable sound: id, family, source type, asset path or synth/MIDI params, bus, priority, voice policy, cooldown, variation, ducking, loop behavior, intended game events, and validation thresholds.
- Build or specify a playback adapter that can route samples, procedural Web Audio builders, and MIDI/rendered clips through the same buses and volume controls.
- Decide how MIDI is supported if selected: either pre-rendered/bundled outputs, a lightweight runtime player, or an authoring-only path. Do not strand the project with a MIDI plan that cannot play in production.
- Add seeded/deterministic options for generated noise and parameter variation where tests need repeatable output.
- Define bus/voice budgets for host, Remote viewer, and high-player scenes: master/music/sfx/engine/ui/weapon/ambience, priority stealing, cooldowns, and distance/importance rules where applicable.
- Preserve browser audio unlock UX and avoid per-frame logging.
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Runtime audit artifact names current audio entry points, loaded assets, hardcoded mappings, placeholder mappings, unlock behavior, and gaps for sample/synth/MIDI/hybrid support.
- Manifest proof: tests or docs define the sound spec schema and include representative entries for engine, boost/wheelie, collision, missile, explosion, pickup, UI, and music/stinger sounds.
- Playback proof: unit/browser-light tests prove sample and procedural sound entries can be resolved and routed through the same volume/bus/cooldown/voice-budget layer; MIDI support is either implemented, pre-rendered, or explicitly authoring-only with no production ambiguity.
- Determinism proof: procedural noise/variation used in tests can be seeded or captured so `OfflineAudioContext` analysis is repeatable.
- Architecture guard: Local phones/controllers do not instantiate unnecessary world audio systems; host owns shared Local presentation, while Remote viewers degrade independently.
- No-regression proof: existing lobby/race/results music, engine synth, collision sounds, and audio unlock behavior still work after the abstraction.

title: SND local sound lab for audition tuning and evidence bundles
description:
Build a local sound lab/debug tool for authoring, auditioning, tuning, and exporting evidence for Joystick Jammers sounds.

Scope:
- Add a local/dev route or equivalent script surface such as `/sound-lab`, following the shared debug-lab pattern used by car viewer, weapon lab, and map tools.
- Load the production sound manifest and let developers audition every sound family without entering a live match.
- Provide controls for parameters that agents often need to tune: envelope, pitch, filter, distortion, noise color, modulation, variation seed, cooldown, bus, ducking, voice priority, loop points, volume, and source type where applicable.
- Render sounds through `OfflineAudioContext` where possible to generate waveform/spectrogram/features without relying only on real-time playback.
- Export an evidence bundle: sound spec JSON, source asset or synth params, rendered WAV/preview where legal and useful, waveform/spectrogram image, feature metrics, A/B comparison notes, and manual listening notes.
- Integrate with weapon/pickup/boost/wheelie use cases so gameplay sounds can be tested in isolation and, where useful, from a weapon-lab or scenario event.
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Normal E2E or browser-light proof: `/sound-lab` or equivalent loads with no console errors, can audition at least sample, procedural synth, and one generated/parameterized sound spec, and exports diagnostics.
- Offline analysis proof: representative sounds render through `OfflineAudioContext` and produce waveform/spectrogram/features in machine-readable artifacts.
- Tuning proof: changing a parameter updates the sound spec, preview, and metrics without touching production code by hand.
- Integration proof: at least one weapon sound, one vehicle-feel sound, one UI sound, and one music/stinger entry can be auditioned from production manifest entries.
- Evidence bundle proof: exported bundle is sufficient for a fresh validator to inspect the sound without rerunning the full game.

title: SND create reusable sound-engineering skill for Claude and Codex
description:
Create a reusable sound-engineering skill for Claude and Codex that applies real sound-design principles while giving agents objective and qualitative feedback loops for sounds they cannot literally hear.

Scope:
- Create a project-scoped skill under `.claude/skills/` and make it discoverable for Codex as well, matching the pattern used by project model-validation skills.
- Keep the skill generic enough to reuse on other game/audio projects, but include a Joystick Jammers adapter section: Local/Remote roles, no-CDN runtime rule, sound manifest, sound lab, bundled assets, and required evidence before closing sound beads.
- Include a concise workflow: derive sound intent, choose source strategy, author/generate, render preview, analyze features, inspect visual/audio artifacts, run rubric, iterate, wire through manifest, validate in gameplay context, then attach evidence.
- Include sound-design principles: transient/body/tail, spectral space, loudness hierarchy, masking, repetition variation, loop points, engine/boost/impact/weapon/UI differences, fatigue, readability in a noisy room, and style matching to the lo-fi/arcade grade.
- Provide LLM feedback techniques: waveform and spectrogram images, feature metrics, A/B comparison tables, descriptive rubrics, failure signatures, and explicit uncertainty/human listening gates.
- Prefer reusable scripts or references for analysis rather than making every agent rewrite audio-analysis code.
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Skill artifact: `.claude/skills/<sound-skill>/SKILL.md` exists with valid frontmatter, concise instructions, and project adapter notes; Codex discoverability/install instructions or symlink support are present where this repo expects them.
- Validation scripts/references: the skill includes or points to reusable scripts/references for rendering/analyzing audio evidence: waveform, spectrogram, duration, peak/RMS/LUFS-like loudness, crest factor, spectral centroid, silence/clipping, and transient envelope where practical.
- Qualitative rubric: skill tells agents how to produce a judgement packet the LLM can reason over and when to escalate to human listening/playtest because metrics are insufficient.
- `quick_validate.py` or equivalent skill validation passes, and at least one dry-run task demonstrates the skill can evaluate a sample/procedural sound artifact without relying on hidden context.
- Scope guard: the skill bans runtime CDN audio dependencies and requires project evidence before closing any new Joystick Jammers sound bead.

title: SND new-sound acceptance gate and existing audio bead wiring
description:
Define and enforce the acceptance gate for adding or changing any gameplay sound after the sound pipeline exists.

Scope:
- Update relevant audio beads and future new-sound work so they cannot close by merely adding an mp3 or oscillator function.
- Require each new/changed sound to have a manifest entry, intent statement, source strategy, validation thresholds, event wiring, bus/priority/voice policy, and evidence bundle.
- Define family-specific checks for engine, boost/wheelie, collision, weapon, pickup, UI, music/stinger, and ambience/tape-hiss sounds.
- Require gameplay-context proof for any sound that communicates state: e.g. boost/wheelie steering authority, missile launch danger, pickup confirmation, collision severity, or results/lobby energy.
- Preserve human taste gate for signature sounds, but make the human review packet concrete: A/B evidence, waveform/spectrogram, metrics, in-game capture, and playtest notes.
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Gate doc or tests define the required checklist for every new/changed sound: intent, source, manifest, bus, voice policy, event wiring, objective metrics, lab evidence, gameplay proof, and human/manual gate when needed.
- Existing relevant beads are wired to the gate: lo-fi audio pass, boost audio ramp, wheelie sound cues, weapon/pickup sounds, and around-couch audio budgets mention or depend on this gate.
- Normal tests or lint checks catch missing manifest entries, broken asset paths, missing procedural builders, invalid bus/priority values, and absent validation metadata.
- E2E or lab evidence shows at least one sample sound and one procedural sound pass through the full gate.
- No taste-only closure: close notes for sound beads must include evidence bundles or a clear manual human listening gate, not just prose that it sounds better.

title: Local weapon test lab separate from the main game
description:
Build a standalone /weapon-lab that mirrors the existing /car-viewer paint/model debug editor: full-screen Three.js viewport, compact inspector panels, production code paths, deterministic reset/step hooks, visual overlays, parameter tuning, screenshots, and machine-readable diagnostics.

Background:
Weapons currently require a live room and normal match flow to test pickups, fire events, projectiles, deployables, buffs, hitscan, AOE, zones, continuous effects, damage, VFX, and HUD notifications. That slows iteration and hides bugs. The lab must exercise the real WeaponSystem and production track/arena data, not a mocked duplicate.

Scope:
- Add a dev route such as /weapon-lab with Vite/server wiring similar to host/player/car-viewer surfaces.
- Instantiate real WeaponSystem, with fake render/damage adapters only where needed to drive the production behavior under a local scenario.
- Load production tracks/arenas through TrackFactory so spawn areas, pickup zones, barriers, ramps, and surfaces match game behavior.
- Provide presets: pickup field, missile chase, mine arming, oil slick, rail gun, EMP blast, shield/boost, flamethrower cone, and crowd stress.
- Show overlays for spawn areas, pickup positions, projectile paths, mine trigger/explosion radii, hitscan lines, AOE radii, oil zones, flamethrower cones, target health, stun/buff timers, shield state, inventory, and emitted weapon events.
- Provide tunables for projectile speed/lifetime/tracking, mine arm delay/radius, buff duration/multipliers, hitscan range/aim tolerance, AOE radius/stun duration, zone radius/friction/lifetime, continuous cone/range/tick rate/damage, damage/falloff, progression weights, spawn interval, and max active pickups.
- Provide Reset, Step 1 frame, Step 1 sec, Play/Pause, Screenshot, Export JSON, Import JSON, and Run checks.
- Scenario JSON begins with schema jj.weaponLabScenario.v1 and records seed, trackId, preset, arenaConfigPatch, weaponOverrides, actors, and camera/overlay settings.
- Use GameRunContext clock/rng once available. No Math.random/performance.now patching as the final design.
- Expose window.__weaponLab with reset, step, screenshot, runChecks, and getDiagnostics for tests.
- Avoid per-frame console spam; use explicit event log and diagnostics export.

Acceptance criteria:
- A developer can select every current weapon type, tune uncertain parameters, run a preset, step deterministically, and export an evidence bundle.
- The lab exercises the same WeaponSystem behavior used by the game for spawn, pickup, fire, hit, buff/stun, projectile/effect lifetime, cleanup, and emitted events.
- Unit tests cover built-in weapon definitions, deterministic pickup selection/placement, preset reset/step/cleanup, missile hit, mine arm/explode, oil friction toggle/clear, shield block, EMP stun, and flamethrower cone damage.
- Playwright covers /weapon-lab load with no console errors, nonblank canvas, screenshot hook, Run checks pass badges, and deterministic preset stepping.
- The lab follows the shared debug-lab pattern and safely renders all text labels/diagnostics.
acceptance_criteria:
Evidence required before close:
- Normal unit CI: `npx vitest run tests/unit/weapon-definitions.test.js tests/unit/weapon-lab-scenarios.test.js tests/unit/weapon-system-determinism.test.js` covers definitions, deterministic pickup selection/placement, preset reset/step/cleanup, missile hit, mine arm/explode, oil friction toggle/clear, shield block, EMP stun, and flamethrower cone damage.
- Normal E2E: `npx playwright test tests/e2e/weapon-lab.spec.ts tests/e2e/console-errors.spec.ts` proves `/weapon-lab` loads with a nonblank canvas, no console errors, screenshot hook works, diagnostics are machine-readable, and Run checks shows pass/fail badges.
- Production-path proof: diagnostics must name the real WeaponSystem and TrackFactory code paths used; fake adapters may provide render/damage harnesses only and must not duplicate weapon rules.
- Determinism proof: same scenario JSON seed/preset/overrides produces the same pickup schedule, projectile/effect lifetimes, emitted event log, and diagnostics hash across two resets.
- Evidence bundle: screenshot, exported scenario JSON, weapon overrides, event log, diagnostics JSON, and any changed tunables.

title: Host WebGPU createRenderer failure falls back to WebGL
description:
Focused split from br-captain-call-architecture-hardening-woq.2. Chrome/WebGPU-capable hosts selected WebGPUBackend, but WebGPUBackend.createRenderer() still throws while native WebGPU rendering is stubbed. RenderSystem treated that creation-time throw as fatal, showing the host crash overlay instead of falling back to WebGL. Scope of this bead is only the operational-safety fallback: when WebGPU creation fails, create and report a WebGL fallback renderer with an explicit fallback reason, without pretending the broader first-class-WebGPU acceptance is complete.

Acceptance criteria / evidence required before close:
- Unit/browser-light CI covers WebGPU creation failure -> WebGL fallback, option forwarding, diagnostics/name/fallback reason, helper-level fallback, forceWebGL/kill-switch behavior, and terminal failure when WebGL itself fails.
- Build check: npm run build passes with the fallback source included in dist.
- Browser proof: in Chromium with navigator.gpu forced/present, /host loads without crash overlay, creates a WebGL2 renderer, and renders a nonblank canvas while logging/reporting the WebGPU fallback reason.
- Scope guard: br-captain-call-architecture-hardening-woq.2 remains open for real native WebGPU path, adapter/device limits, race/derby parity, performance diagnostics, and architecture-guard evidence.
close_reason: Completed and independently validated: WebGPU createRenderer failure falls back to WebGL — unit 9/9, npm run build green with fallback bundled in dist, and /host with navigator.gpu forced present loads with no crash overlay, creates 'WebGL (WebGPU fallback)', renders a nonblank canvas, and logs the fallback reason. Parent woq.2 left open.

title: Fix engine.io payload limit under 32-controller soak
description:
During br-fb-spawncap-qi9 validation, both the implementer and fresh validator reproduced repeated `ERROR:engineio.server: ValueError: Too many packets in payload` under `JJ_SOAK_PLAYERS=32 npx playwright test tests/e2e/around-couch-soak.spec.ts --reporter=line`. The spawn assertions still passed with distinct non-overlapping spawns, so this is not a spawn-placement blocker, but it is a transport/load risk for the no-caps multiplayer goal.

Scope: investigate Socket.IO/engine.io packet batching and controller update rate under 32+ controllers; determine whether to raise safe engine.io payload/packet limits, reduce burst packet counts, change transport settings, or throttle initial join/start/control bursts. Preserve Local phones-as-controllers architecture and Remote graceful-degrade behavior.

Acceptance: a 32-controller soak completes without engine.io Too many packets in payload errors in server logs; add a regression test/log assertion or documented soak evidence; include a recommendation for 60-player target limits and any safe server config changes.
close_reason: Completed and fresh-validated: 32-controller soak now uses explicit hybrid socket transport override, preserving polling-only testMode defaults; npm build, 32-player soak with no engine.io payload errors, and join-flow rerun passed.

comments.author: CodexPayloadSoak
comments.text:
CLAIMED (CodexPayloadSoak). Agent Mail MCP unavailable -> coordinating here. No new panes.

CURRENT STATE / CONFLICT GUARD
- br-around-couch-risk-resolution-3xv.16 was open/unclaimed; claimed now.
- br-around-couch-risk-resolution-3xv.4 is actively planning surgical server/app.py edits for input security. I am not editing server/app.py yet. If the eventual fix needs SocketIO init wiring there, I will coordinate first instead of overlapping.
- Dirty tree is large; preserving all unrelated visual/rendering WIP.

INITIAL FILE SCOPE (investigation-first, no edits yet)
- tests/e2e/around-couch-soak.spec.ts
- tests/e2e/fixtures.ts
- static/js/player.js
- static/js/systems/NetworkSystem.js
- possible new transport helper under server/ if we need server-side tuning later
- server/app.py only after coordination clearance from 3xv.4, because that file is already active

REPRO / EXACT SOAK EVIDENCE
1. Exact bead reproduction command:
   JJ_SOAK_PLAYERS=32 npx playwright test tests/e2e/around-couch-soak.spec.ts --reporter=line
   Result: TEST PASS in 45.7s, but server logs reproduced the bug immediately after start:
   - INFO:__main__:Game started in room JOZD
   - repeated ERROR:engineio.server:post request handler error
   - repeated ValueError: Too many packets in payload
   Stack observed from engineio.server.handle_request -> engineio.socket.handle_post_request -> engineio.payload.Payload.decode.
2. Read-only transport diagnosis:
   - tests/e2e/fixtures.ts drives host/player to /host?testMode=1 and /player?testMode=1
   - static/js/player.js and static/js/systems/NetworkSystem.js both map testMode=1 to transports: ['polling']
   - python-engineio 4.11.0 in this env exposes Payload.max_decode_packets = 16
3. Read-only comparison run without testMode=1 (inline Playwright/Node script against /host and /player, websocket upgrade allowed):
   Result: 32 controllers joined, game started, and I did NOT observe any Too many packets in payload errors in server logs.
   Numeric evidence from that run:
   - vehicleCount: 32
   - generatedSpawnCount: 64
   - spawnPairs.minDistance: 3.5207840535802526
   - currentPairs.minDistance: 4.04068439701935
   - roomCode: XIOA
   Server log during that comparison showed normal join/start/disconnect lines only, no engineio payload-limit tracebacks.

WORKING THEORY
- This is a forced-polling burst problem, not a spawn-placement problem.
- Under testMode=1 every host/player socket stays on HTTP polling; when 32 controllers begin streaming together after start_game, engine.io batches more than 16 packets into a POST body and rejects it.
- The websocket-enabled comparison staying clean makes the main recommendation path much narrower.

ACCEPTANCE PLAN
- Reproduce-free fix path must make JJ_SOAK_PLAYERS=32 npx playwright test tests/e2e/around-couch-soak.spec.ts --reporter=line complete with zero Too many packets in payload errors in server logs.
- If I touch JS/browser code: npm run build before any browser/E2E rerun.
- Add a regression guard: either a targeted test for transport selection / burst control, or a documented soak log assertion path that proves the server stayed clean.
- Edge cases I will deliberately cover:
  - ordinary E2E specs that currently rely on testMode stability still connect reliably
  - high-controller local mode keeps canonical phone-controller architecture
  - reconnect/disconnect cleanup still works under whichever transport path we keep
  - if polling-only remains supported, initial start/control bursts stay under packet limits

RECOMMENDATION PATH
1. Preferred: stop forcing polling-only for the 32-controller soak path. Make transport mode selectable so routine E2E can keep any needed polling workaround, but scale/soak validation can allow websocket upgrades.
2. Fallback / defense-in-depth if polling-only must remain valid at 32+: add explicit engine.io packet-budget tuning on the server side and prove it with the same soak plus clean server logs.
3. I will avoid server/app.py until 3xv.4 coordination clears overlap; likely first implementation work is in the test fixture + client transport selection, with server tuning only if needed afterward.

No edits yet. Starting from the transport-selection path because the reproduction evidence points there more strongly than a broad server rewrite.

comments.text:
READY FOR FRESH VALIDATION

Implementation summary
- Avoided server/app.py overlap entirely while br-around-couch-risk-resolution-3xv.4 is active.
- Added an explicit socket transport override path on host/player clients and Playwright helpers.
- Kept default testMode=1 behavior unchanged for existing E2E stability (polling-only unless overridden).
- Switched only the 32-controller soak to socketTransport=hybrid so it keeps testMode shortcuts but exercises the production-style websocket-upgrade path.

Touched files
- tests/e2e/fixtures.ts
- tests/e2e/around-couch-soak.spec.ts
- static/js/player.js
- static/js/systems/NetworkSystem.js

Exact evidence run
1. npm run build
   PASS. Existing non-fatal warnings remained:
   - /static/js/audioManager.js in frontend/host/index.html cannot be bundled without type=module
   - standard Vite chunk-size warnings
2. JJ_SOAK_PLAYERS=32 npx playwright test tests/e2e/around-couch-soak.spec.ts --reporter=line
   PASS: 1 passed (38.5s)
   Observed server log after patch:
   - Room created: KREB
   - Player Soak1..Soak32 joined
   - Game started in room KREB
   - controller disconnect cleanup on teardown
   - Host disconnected, room KREB entering host-loss grace
   Critically: NO ERROR:engineio.server lines and NO ValueError: Too many packets in payload tracebacks on this run.
3. npx playwright test tests/e2e/join-flow.spec.ts --workers=1
   PASS: 8 passed (8.8s)
   This confirms the existing default polling-pinned testMode path still works after the override plumbing.

What changed behaviorally
- testMode=1 no longer hard-locks every scenario to polling if socketTransport is explicitly supplied.
- The around-couch soak now uses testMode=1&socketTransport=hybrid, so it preserves countdown/test shortcuts while allowing websocket upgrades and avoiding the engine.io 16-packet polling payload cap reproduced earlier.
- Outside explicit overrides, product/default behavior is unchanged.

Recommendation / 60-player guidance
- Treat hybrid/websocket transport as the scale target for 32+ and future 60-player validation.
- Treat polling-only as a compatibility path, not the no-caps soak path.
- If a future requirement says 60 players must also survive forced polling-only, that should be a follow-up server-side packet-budget/transport-tuning bead with dedicated telemetry, because python-engineio in this env still exposes Payload.max_decode_packets = 16.

Residual risks
- I did not change server-side packet limits, so an explicitly forced polling-only 32-controller run can still reproduce the old engine.io payload error.
- No automated server-log assertion was added beyond the documented soak evidence above.
- Validation is against the current dirty tree; unrelated WIP was preserved.

Ready for a fresh validator rerun. Do not close from this comment.

Residual risks / notes
- This fix does NOT make forced polling-only a 32-controller-safe path; it reroutes the soak onto the hybrid/websocket-upgrade path.
- I observed a preexisting-looking warning before room creation in both soak/join-flow runs: `Host reclaim rejected for ONVA: invalid token`. It did not block either suite and was unrelated to the engine.io payload-limit error acceptance check, but it is worth keeping an eye on separately.
- There is still no automated assertion over server logs inside the Playwright test itself; the regression proof here is the explicit soak rerun plus captured log inspection.

Ready for coordinator close.

title: Room code: click QR/text to copy + fix Cmd+C copy
description:
USER-REPORTED BUG + UX: Cmd+C does not copy the join room code, and there is no click-to-copy affordance.

Ask: (1) fix keyboard copy (Cmd/Ctrl+C) of the room code; (2) make BOTH the QR image and the room-code text clickable to copy the join URL/code immediately; (3) show visual feedback on copy (e.g. 'Copied!' toast / checkmark / brief highlight).

Grounded start points: RoomCodeOverlayUI.js and LobbyUI.js render the code + QR (see the QR visibility state machine from br-fb-qr-overlay-zf5). The copy target should be the full join URL (what the QR encodes), not just the short code.
acceptance_criteria:
BEHAVIORAL ACCEPTANCE (observable, not unit-only):
- Clicking the QR image copies the join URL to clipboard; clicking the room-code text copies it too.
- Cmd+C / Ctrl+C with the code focused (or nothing else selected) copies the join URL.
- On every successful copy, visible feedback appears within ~100ms (toast/checkmark/highlight) and clears after a couple seconds.
- Works in lobby AND in-progress overlay states without reintroducing the HUD-overlap fixed in br-fb-qr-overlay-zf5.

EVIDENCE REQUIRED BEFORE CLOSE:
- Playwright test that clicks the QR and the text, asserts clipboard content == join URL, and asserts the feedback element becomes visible then hides.
- Screenshot/gif of the copied-state feedback.

ANTI-NARROWING CLAUSE: Done only when copy works from the real host page via BOTH click targets AND keyboard, with visible feedback. A unit test of a copy() helper is necessary but NOT sufficient.

SOURCE: user feedback 2026-07-03 (this session); relates br-fb-qr-overlay-zf5.

title: Host can kick a car / force reconnection via small menu UX
description:
USER-REPORTED, GENUINELY UNCOVERED GROUND (not in the 29-item feedback set or any plan doc): the host needs a way to KICK a specific car, forcing that player's controller to reconnect/rejoin.

Ask: a small, discoverable menu UX path (host screen) listing connected players/cars, each with a 'kick' action that removes the car and forces the phone to re-pair/rejoin. Useful for stuck cars, griefers, ghost/DC'd cars, and freeing seats.

Should coordinate with the room-seat registry + rejoin work (br-around-couch-risk-resolution-3xv.2 seats; br-captain-call-architecture-hardening-woq.13 rejoin) so a kicked seat is cleanly released and re-joinable.
design: Approach: host-authoritative kick event over the socket protocol -> server invalidates that seat's session/token -> car despawns -> phone is bounced to the join/rejoin screen. Surface via a compact host-side player list menu (extend existing host UI, not a new full screen). Reuse seat-release semantics from the room-seat registry rather than inventing a parallel path.
acceptance_criteria:
BEHAVIORAL ACCEPTANCE (observable):
- Host can open a player/car list and trigger 'kick' on a specific player in <=2 interactions.
- Kicked car despawns immediately; that player's phone is sent to the rejoin/join flow (not left frozen).
- The freed seat is immediately re-joinable (same or different device) with no ghost car left behind.
- Kicking during an active race/derby does not corrupt results/ranking state.

EVIDENCE REQUIRED BEFORE CLOSE:
- Playwright/multi-client test: 3 players join, host kicks player 2, assert player-2 car removed + player-2 client shows rejoin UI + seat re-joinable.
- Manual/vision playtest artifact of the menu path.

ANTI-NARROWING CLAUSE: Done only when the full loop (kick -> despawn -> client bounced -> seat reusable) works in the running game. A server-side kick handler with a unit test is necessary but NOT sufficient.

SOURCE: user feedback 2026-07-03 (this session). Coordinates with br-around-couch-risk-resolution-3xv.2, br-captain-call-architecture-hardening-woq.13.
